items.title: Content & Social Media Manager
items.description:
Laundryheap is an award-winning, industry-leading startup transforming the laundry and dry cleaning industry. With services across 14 global markets and expanding rapidly across Europe, Asia, and North America, we pride ourselves on delivering clean clothes within 24 hours, making us one of the fastest services in the business.

Role
We are looking for a Content & Social Media Manager to own content creation, blog and SEO content, and social media management across platforms. This role will focus on performance-driven content that supports SEO growth, website traffic, customer acquisition, and engagement across social channels.

This is a hands-on role responsible for writing, planning, and distributing content across website and social media platforms, while working closely with the SEO, Brand, Design, and Marketing teams.

Key Responsibilities

1. Content & SEO Content Writing

  • Own and manage blog content and SEO content creation
  • Write blog articles, landing page content, city/service pages, and website copy
  • Work closely with the SEO team on keyword-focused content
  • Refresh and optimise existing website content
  • Ensure all website content is SEO-friendly and conversion-focused
  • Support backlink/PR content where required
  • Maintain a blog/content calendar aligned with SEO and marketing priorities

2. Social Media Management

  • Manage and grow the company’s presence across:
    Instagram|
    Facebook
    LinkedIn
    Pinterest
    TikTok

Responsibilities include:

  • Creating monthly social media content calendars
  • Writing captions and post copy
  • Planning post topics and content themes
  • Working with the design team on creatives/collateral
  • Publishing and scheduling posts
  • Monitoring engagement and performance
  • Growing followers and engagement
  • Identifying trends and content opportunities

3. Video Content & Scripts

  • Plan and develop short-form video content ideas
  • Write scripts for Reels, TikTok, and short-form videos
  • Work with designers/video editors to produce videos
  • Develop content around lifestyle, tips, customer stories, convenience, and brand messaging
  • Identify video trends and content formats that can drive engagement and reach

4. Influencer & Collaboration Content

  • Identify and reach out to relevant influencers and content creators
  • Support influencer collaborations and content campaigns
  • Plan influencer content topics and messaging
  • Track influencer content performance and engagement
  • Help build partnerships for social media growth and brand visibility

5. Content Planning & Campaign Support

  • Develop content calendars aligned with marketing campaigns, promotions, and seasonal events
  • Create content to support marketing campaigns and offers
  • Support paid media team with ad copy and messaging where required
  • Repurpose blog and website content into social media content
  • Ensure consistent messaging across website and social channels

KPIs / Success Metrics

Performance will be measured based on:

  • Blog content production and website content delivered
  • Organic traffic to blog and content pages
  • Social media follower growth
  • Engagement rates on social media
  • Video content production and performance
  • Content delivered for campaigns and promotions
  • Influencer collaborations and content output
  • Contribution of content to marketing and SEO initiatives

Experience & Requirements

  • 5–7 years of experience in content marketing, social media, or digital marketing
  • Strong content writing and copywriting skills (long form and short form)
  • Experience writing blog and SEO content
  • Experience managing social media accounts for a brand
  • Experience creating content calendars and content strategies
  • Experience working with designers and marketing teams
  • Understanding of SEO content and digital marketing
  • Experience with short-form video content planning and scripting
  • Experience working with influencers or content creators is a strong plus
  • Highly organised and able to manage multiple projects and deadlines

Tools / Skills (Preferred)

  • Social media scheduling tools
  • Google Analytics / Search Console (basic understanding)
  • Canva or basic content tools
  • CMS platforms (WordPress or similar)
  • Basic understanding of SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, etc.)

Reporting Structure
This role will report to the Organic and Content Marketing Head and will work closely with SEO, Design, and Brand teams.

Work Schedule

  • UK Shift: 9-hour shift (8 working hours
  • 5 days a week (Mon-Fri)

If you’re a creative, performance-driven content marketer who thrives on storytelling, data-backed decision-making, and building engaging brand experiences across digital platforms, this is your opportunity to shape Laundryheap’s global voice and drive meaningful growth through content.

items.title: Social and Community Manager: JioBlast
items.description:
JioBLAST is a joint venture between Jio, RISE Worldwide and the Danish esports company BLAST ApS, officially launched in October 2025. The partnership's goal is to grow and shape the esports industry in India by creating world-class tournaments and competitive entertainment, leveraging Jio's digital ecosystem, BLAST's global esports expertise, and RISE's domestic sports experience.

Welcome to Jio BLAST and the Audience Team!

As the Social Media and Community Manager, you’ll be the voice and pulse of JioBLAST online: driving conversations, fandom, and engagement across creators, gamers, and brands. You’ll craft digital narratives that make people feel something, build online communities that belong, and turn moments into movements.

We're here to build new entertainment products with the biggest games publishers and brands in the world with the number one goal of entertaining India's youth and creating new heroes.

At JioBLAST, our company values guide everything we do:

We Make It Bold by experimenting and pushing boundaries

We Make It Together by empowering each other and solving challenges as a team

And we Make It Happen by taking ownership and delivering with precision

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop and execute a cohesive social media strategy across all key platforms, aligned with brand campaigns, publisher partnerships, and IPs.
  • Manage daily social content calendars, ensuring timely, consistent, and culturally relevant communication that resonates with youth audiences.
  • Collaborate with creators, internal teams, and agencies to ideate and deliver engaging social activations, short-form videos, and real-time coverage during live events or tournaments.
  • Build and lead the JioBLAST community ecosystem. Act as the bridge between the audience and internal teams, bringing community insights into brand and content planning, and driving community-level activations across IPs.
  • Own and maintain the brand voice online, positioning JioBLAST at the center of gaming and youth culture.
  • Track and analyze social performance metrics to measure content impact, engagement, and community sentiment, and share actionable insights with key stakeholders.
  • Work cross-functionally with the Live Operations, Partnerships, and Creative teams to amplify ongoing campaigns, IP launches, and creator collaborations.
  • Align with event and production teams to integrate social storytelling into the overall brand experience before, during, and after live shows.
  • This position involves travel as part of the role. Candidates must be willing and able to travel as required.
  • 3–6 years of experience managing social media for youth, gaming, entertainment, or creator-led brands.
  • Proven track record of growing online communities and leading high-engagement social campaigns.
  • Deep understanding of Gen-Z internet culture, meme language, and creator economy.
  • Hands-on experience with tools like Hubspot, Meta Suite, YouTube Studio, Discord moderation, and social analytics / listening tools.
  • Excellent copywriting and trendspotting skills; bilingual fluency (English + Hinglish) preferred.
  • Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and deliver under tight timelines.
  • Passion for gaming, esports, or pop culture is a major plus.

Experience

  • Experience managing social channels with 100K+ followers and building creator partnerships
  • Exposure to live events or gaming is an advantage.
  • Prior work in start-up, agency, or media house environments preferred.

Benefits

Competitive compensation and employee benefits

How to Apply?

We consider every application and will respond as soon as possible. We are committed to equal opportunities.

items.title: Senior Analyst, Business Intelligence
items.description:
Summary:

The Senior Business Intelligence Analyst plays a pivotal role in driving data-driven decision-making across the organization. This role is responsible for developing dynamic dashboards, delivering actionable insights, and ensuring the effective use of analytics tools to support strategic objectives.

Responsibilities:

  • Develop, maintain, and enhance Amazon Quicksight dashboards and reports to provide visibility into key business metrics.
  • Analyze GTM data to deliver insights that improve decision-making and drive business outcomes.
  • Create robust data models to streamline reporting and ensure alignment with strategic objectives.
  • Design, implement, and manage weekly, monthly, and quarterly scorecards and forecasts for leadership.
  • Partner with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Experience teams to ensure reporting aligns with business needs.
  • Lead the administration and enhancement of Quicksight and other BI platforms to ensure scalability and usability.
  • Provide provocative presentations to key stakeholders to advance understanding and strategy through compelling visuals.
  • Educate GTM teams on how to use BI tools effectively, fostering a data-driven culture.
  • Develop standardized reports and packages that provide field teams with the insights they need to succeed.
  • Respond to requests for deep-dive analyses to uncover trends, opportunities, or risks.

Required Skills & Experience:

  • 4+ years in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a related field, with hands-on expertise in AWS Quicksight.
  • Proficiency in SQL, Python, dbt, data modeling, data visualization, and advanced Excel/Google Sheets for modeling and analysis.
  • Experience with AWS Quicksight/Athena/S3/Lambda, Gitlab, VSCode, Salesforce CRM, Google Sheets, Google Slides.
  • Preferred experience with Fivetran.
  • Strong ability to interpret data, identify trends, and translate findings into actionable strategies.
  • Exceptional attention to detail and a knack for solving complex business problems.
  • Ability to clearly convey insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Analytics, or a relevant/equivalent field.
  • Working knowledge of SaaS metrics (Bookings, Pipeline, Churn, NRR, GRR, LTV, ARR, CAC, etc.) and Revenue Forecasting.
  • Bonus points for experience with other BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) and domain expertise in revenue enablement, GTM/sales operations, professional services reporting, customer intelligence, or churn prediction models.

items.title: IT Business Analyst - IRRBB & Liquidity Risk
items.description:
Experience

8–15 years (flexible based on profile strength)

Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced IT Business Analyst with strong domain expertise in Interest Rate Risk in the Banking Book (IRRBB) and Liquidity Risk to support the design, implementation, and enhancement of risk management systems and regulatory reporting solutions. The role requires close collaboration with Risk, Treasury, Finance, and Technology teams to translate regulatory and business requirements into effective IT solutions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Act as a bridge between Business (Risk/Treasury) and Technology teams for IRRBB and Liquidity initiatives.
  • Gather, analyze, and document business and regulatory requirements related to IRRBB (EVE, NII) and Liquidity Risk (LCR, NSFR, ALM metrics).
  • Translate business requirements into functional specifications, data requirements, and process flows.
  • Support implementation and enhancement of risk calculation engines, ALM systems, data marts, and regulatory reporting platforms.
  • Work closely with developers, architects, and QA teams throughout the SDLC (Agile/Waterfall).
  • Validate system outputs, perform UAT coordination, and support defect resolution.
  • Ensure solutions comply with regulatory guidelines (Basel III/IV, BCBS 368, local regulator requirements).
  • Analyze upstream/downstream data flows, source systems, and data quality issues.
  • Support production issues, change requests, and continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Prepare clear documentation including BRDs, FRDs, user stories, test cases, and traceability matrices.

Required Skills & Experience

Domain Knowledge

  • Strong hands-on experience in IRRBB:
    • EVE and NII calculations
    • Behavioral modeling (NMDs, prepayments)
    • Yield curve construction and interest rate scenarios
  • Strong understanding of Liquidity Risk:
    • LCR, NSFR, cash flow gaps
    • Funding concentration, stress testing
    • ALM concepts
  • Good knowledge of Treasury & Balance Sheet Management concepts.

Business Analysis & IT Skills

  • Proven experience as an IT Business Analyst in banking/financial services.
  • Strong requirement elicitation and documentation skills.
  • Experience working with risk systems, ALM platforms, or vendor solutions (e.g., QRM, Murex, Moody’s, FIS, in-house platforms).
  • Solid understanding of data models, SQL, and data lineage.
  • Experience with Agile (JIRA, Confluence) and/or Waterfall methodologies.
  • Ability to work with large datasets and complex calculations.

Regulatory Knowledge

  • Basel III / IV
  • IRRBB (BCBS 368)
  • Liquidity Risk standards (LCR, NSFR)

Familiarity with local regulatory reporting requirements is a plus

items.title: Senior Analyst- Global Business Operations
items.description:
InMobi Advertising is a global technology leader helping marketers win the moments that matter. Our advertising platform reaches over 2 billion people across 150+ countries and turns real-time context into business outcomes, delivering results grounded in privacy-first principles. Trusted by 30,000+ brands and leading publishers, InMobi is where intelligence, creativity, and accountability converge. By combining lock screens, apps, TVs, and the open web with AI and machine learning, we deliver receptive attention, precise personalization, and measurable impact.

Through Glance AI, we are shaping AI Commerce, reimagining the future of e-commerce with inspiration-led discovery and shopping. Designed to seamlessly integrate into everyday consumer technology, Glance AI transforms every screen into a gateway for instant, personal, and joyful discovery.  Spanning diverse categories such as fashion, beauty, travel, accessories, home décor, pets, and beyond, Glance AI delivers deeply personalized shopping experiences. With rich first-party data and unparalleled consumer access, it harnesses InMobi’s global scale, insights, and targeting capabilities to create high impact, performance driven shopping journeys for brands worldwide.

Recognized as a Great Place to Work, and by MIT Technology Review, Fast Company’s Top 10 Innovators, and more, InMobi is a workplace where bold ideas create global impact. Backed by investors including SoftBank, Kleiner Perkins, and Sherpalo Ventures, InMobi has offices across San Mateo, New York, London, Singapore, Tokyo, Seoul, Jakarta, Bengaluru and beyond.

At InMobi Advertising, you’ll have the opportunity to shape how billions of users connect with content, commerce, and brands worldwide. To learn more, visit www.inmobi.com

Who are we and What do we do?

InMobi Group’s mission is to power intelligent, mobile-first experiences for enterprises and consumers. Its businesses across advertising, marketing, data and content platforms are shaping consumer experience in a world of connected devices. InMobi Group has been recognized on both the 2018 and 2019 CNBC Disruptor 50 list and as one of Fast Company’s 2018 World’s Most Innovative Companies.

What’s the InMobi family like?

Consistently featured among the “Great Places to Work” in India since 2017, our culture is our true north, enabling us to think big, solve complex challenges and grow with new opportunities. InMobians are passionate and driven, creative and fun-loving, take ownership and are results-focused. We invite you to free yourself, dream big and chase your passion.

What do we promise?

We offer an opportunity to have an immediate impact on the company and our products. The work that you shall do will be mission critical for InMobi and will be critical for optimizing tech operations, working with highly capable and ambitious peer groups. At InMobi, you get food for your body, soul, and mind with daily meals, gym, and yoga classes, cutting-edge training and tools, cocktails at drink cart Thursdays and fun at work on Funky Fridays. We even promise to let you bring your kids and pets to work.

About the Team & Role

Global Business Operations plays a pivotal role in executing and delivering effective campaigns across channels in the global market. The team is the central hub for all campaign-related tasks across every line of business with an unwavering focus on quality, accuracy and stakeholder alignment. From curating client reports and tracking live performance to deriving actionable insights from raw data and resolving platform delivery issues proactively, the team operates as a customer-focused, insight-driven engine that connects operational execution with the company's strategic vision.

As a GBO Senior Analyst, you will be the operational backbone of campaign delivery, managing end-to-end execution across multiple platforms including DSPs, Glance Lockscreen and Glance TV, while building robust reports and interactive dashboards that drive smarter decisions. You will work closely with internal stakeholders, regional teams and clients to ensure flawless campaign performance at every stage.

What we promise: This is a role with immediate, visible impact. Your work will be mission-critical to InMobi's operations, optimizing delivery, shaping client outcomes, and contributing to a team of highly capable and ambitious professionals who take ownership and move fast.

 

Responsibilities

  • Execute end-to-end campaign operations across multiple platforms, including campaign setup, modifications, tracker testing, and screenshot documentation.
  • Manage and optimize campaigns across Glance Lockscreen and Glance TV platforms, ensuring smooth delivery and adherence to client specifications.
  • Generate and deliver campaign performance reports for clients with key insights at launch, and during pre- and post-campaign phases.
  • Extract and consolidate performance data from Mobile Measurement Partners (MMPs) such as AppsFlyer, Adjust, or Branch, alongside internal databases, to build comprehensive and unified performance views.
  • Develop data stories that translate complex datasets into clear, actionable narratives for business teams.
  • Leverage data to identify trends, predict behaviors, and uncover growth opportunities, driving decisions aligned with strategic business goals.
  • Utilize third-party tools to generate internal performance and delivery reports.
  • Take ownership of incoming requests from the team queue, ensuring timely completion within defined SLAs and TATs.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional internal teams including Sales, Account Management, Product, Finance, and Engineering to resolve queries and ensure consistent and efficient campaign delivery.
  • Contribute to cross-functional initiatives involving leadership teams; enhance problem-solving through exposure to multi-disciplinary projects.
  • Support documentation of internal procedures, policies, and tutorials related to campaign setup, delivery, and reporting.
  • Stay updated with process changes and product updates, ensuring quick adaptation and seamless integration into daily workflows.
  • Serve as a point of contact for assigned regional accounts, managing stakeholder expectations and campaign delivery tracking.
  • Assist team lead in providing onboarding and training support for new team members, acting as a resource for knowledge transfer and guidance.

 

Requirements

  • MBA or equivalent degree from a reputable institute with a minimum of 3-4 years’ work experience in Ad Operations, Campaign Management, or a related Analytics role.
  • Good understanding of the online advertising industry and ecosystem, prior exposure to programmatic, mobile advertising, or digital media is preferred.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication and documentation skills with the ability to interact effectively with clients and internal stakeholders.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to derive meaningful insights from large datasets, including descriptive and diagnostic analysis (must have)
  • Proficiency in Advanced MS Excel, PowerPoint, and SQL for querying internal databases.
  • Hands-on experience building dashboards and reports in visualization tools like Power BI, Rill, Tableau or equivalent.
  • Experience with MMP platforms such as AppsFlyer, Adjust, or Branch for data extraction and attribution reporting.
  • Data storytelling ability, translating numbers into meaningful business narratives for both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Quick learner with high accuracy and the ability to manage high work volumes effectively in a fast-paced environment.
  • Self-motivated, collaborative team player with a demonstrated ability to work across cross-functional teams.
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize and manage multiple projects simultaneously under pressure.
  • Flexible to work in shifts as per business requirements and adaptable to extended timelines as project demands.
  • Results-driven with a passion for performance and a desire to stay current on industry trends and technologies.

 

The InMobi Culture

At InMobi, culture isn’t a buzzword; it's an ethos woven by every InMobian, reflecting our diverse backgrounds and experiences.

We thrive on challenges and seize every opportunity for growth. Our core values — thinking big, being passionate, showing accountability, and taking ownership with freedom — guide us in every decision we make.

We believe in nurturing and investing in your development through continuous learning and career progression with our InMobi Live Your Potential program.

InMobi is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities throughout the hiring process and in the workplace.

Visit https://www.inmobi.com/company/careers to better understand our benefits, values, and more!

items.title: Business Analyst (4 to 6 Years) Merchant
items.description:
About PhonePe Limited:

Headquartered in India, its flagship product, the PhonePe digital payments app, was launched in Aug 2016. As of April 2025, PhonePe has over 60 Crore (600 Million) registered users and a digital payments acceptance network spread across over 4 Crore (40+ million) merchants. PhonePe also processes over 33 Crore (330+ Million) transactions daily with an Annualized Total Payment Value (TPV) of over INR 150 lakh crore. 

 

PhonePe’s portfolio of businesses includes the distribution of financial products (Insurance, Lending, and Wealth) as well as new consumer tech businesses (Pincode - hyperlocal e-commerce and Indus AppStore Localized App Store for the Android ecosystem) in India, which are aligned with the company’s vision to offer every Indian an equal opportunity to accelerate their progress by unlocking the flow of money and access to services.

 

Culture:

At PhonePe, we go the extra mile to make sure you can bring your best self to work, Everyday!. And that starts with creating the right environment for you. We empower people and trust them to do the right  thing. Here, you own your work from start to finish, right from day one. PhonePe-rs solve complex problems and execute quickly; often building frameworks from scratch. If you’re excited by the idea of building platforms that touch millions, ideating with some of  the best minds in the country and executing on your dreams with purpose and speed, join us!

Experience Bucket: 4 to 6 Years

What kind of person are we looking for?
First-principle problem solvers who are passionate about working with data and uncovering the stories that the numbers hide. If you’re a curious mind and constantly questions the status-quo, then you’d fit right in with us.

What would you get if you work with us?
You’ll be closely working on problem statements and influencing decisions that impact 60 crore + people; if you’re not keeping track. You’ll get to interact with some of the smartest professionals that the country has to offer and get exposure to all facets of building a product at scale. You get complete ownership and responsibility of all analytics support for your area right from identifying the problem to presenting to the CEO, if need be.

What would you get to do in this role?

  • Be an integral part of the pod strategy and will have to define the critical metrics. You’ll also monitor performance trends and influence any interventions, based on performance.
  • Collaborate closely with the business, and product functions to understand the business problem and translate them into the right analytical problem statement.
  • Own the entire insight generation phase and work on deep, thorough analysis to provide unbiased answers on the identified problem statement. These insights and decisions will be influenced based on your presentation of the evidence, backed by a data-driven hypothesis.
  • Move past just being the “Data person” and contribute with individual thoughts on how to improve the critical metrics for the pod. We love people who think of themselves as the business owner and then go back to the data to prove or disprove the thought.
  • Act as the analytics and visualization SME for the team and ensure that the dashboards are designed and built in a way that makes it easy for the pod to consume the data they need.
  • Mentor other analysts in the team; play an active role in hiring process & new team members’ onboarding process

What do you need to have to apply for this position?

  • Minimum 4 years of analytics experience in relevant roles.
  • Strong problem solving & analytical skills.
  • Penchant for business & curiosity to understand how the product works.
  • Ability to write complex queries on SQL to manipulate, consolidate multiple data sources for the purpose of dashboarding and analysis.
  • Intuition for data and ability to handle big data sources.
  • Strong working knowledge in Excel and visualization tools like PowerBI, Tableau, QlikView.
  • Understanding of data-analysis languages such as R, Python and in core statistical concepts is expected; Experience in building ML models is good-to-have, but not mandatory. Ability to clearly explain thoughts and ideas either verbally or in the written form.
  • Candidates who are able to explain the story behind their analysis will find themselves at an advantage.

PhonePe Full Time Employee Benefits (Not applicable for Intern or Contract Roles)

  • Insurance Benefits - Medical Insurance, Critical Illness Insurance, Accidental Insurance, Life Insurance
  • Wellness Program - Employee Assistance Program, Onsite Medical Center, Emergency Support System
  • Parental Support - Maternity Benefit, Paternity Benefit Program, Adoption Assistance Program, Day-care Support Program
  • Mobility Benefits - Relocation benefits, Transfer Support Policy, Travel Policy
  • Retirement Benefits - Employee PF Contribution, Flexible PF Contribution, Gratuity, NPS, Leave Encashment 
  • Other Benefits - Higher Education Assistance, Car Lease, Salary Advance Policy

 

Our inclusive culture promotes individual expression, creativity, innovation, and achievement and in turn helps us better understand and serve our customers. We see ourselves as a place for intellectual curiosity,  ideas and debates, where diverse perspectives lead to deeper understanding and better quality results. PhonePe is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to treating all its employees and job applicants equally; regardless of  gender, sexual preference, religion, race, color or disability. If you have a disability or special need that requires assistance or reasonable accommodation, during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please fill out this form.

Read more about PhonePe on our blog.

Life at PhonePe

PhonePe in the news

items.title: Business Analyst I/II - Insurance platform
items.description:
WHO WE ARE: 

Zinnia is the leading technology platform for accelerating life and annuities growth. With innovative enterprise solutions and data insights, Zinnia simplifies the experience of buying, selling, and administering insurance products. All of which enables more people to protect their financial futures. Our success is driven by a commitment to three core values: be bold, team up, deliver value – and that we do. Zinnia has over $180 billion in assets under administration, serves 100+ carrier clients, 2500 distributors and partners, and over 2 million policyholders.

 


WHO YOU ARE:
We’re looking for a Senior Business Analyst with 5+ years of experience who thrives on solving problems,eliminating ineƯiciencies, and improving processes through automation. You’re detail-oriented, analytical,and equally comfortable rolling up your sleeves to troubleshoot issues or stepping back to guide and mentor others. In this role, you’ll partner closely with Product and Operations to automate transaction workflows, uncover bugs, support root cause analysis, and may also lead a small team of Business Analysts to drive continuous improvement.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
• Partner with Product & Operations to identify manual, repetitive transaction processes that can beautomated
• Document business requirements and functional specifications for automation of transaction flows
• Analyze and investigate system/data issues, supporting bug identification and resolution tracking
• Collaborate with Engineering to ensure business needs are accurately translated into automated solutions
• Support UAT and validate outcomes for automation fixes or improvements
• Create documentation, workflows, and SOPs for automated processes
• Use data to measure automation success, identify gaps, and recommend improvements
• Write and run basic SQL queries to support analysis, validation, and troubleshooting
• Mentor and guide junior Business Analysts; may manage a small BA team

WHAT YOU’LL NEED:
• Bachelor’s/Master’s degree with 6+ years of experience as a Business Analyst (Insurance, BFSI, or SaaS domain experience preferred)
• Prior exposure to business process automation, workflow tools, or transaction automation initiatives
• Excellent analytical and investigative skills; ability to dig into issues and identify patterns
• Strong experience in bug tracking, triaging, and cross-functional collaboration with QA/Engineering teams
• Familiarity with tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Excel/Sheets, and process mapping platforms
• Clear communication skills for working across business and technical teams
• Basic SQL skills for data extraction, validation, and analysis
• Leadership skills with the ability to mentor, coach, and manage junior team members.

WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

We’re looking for the best and brightest innovators in the industry to join our team. At Zinnia, you collaborate with smart, creative professionals who are dedicated to delivering cutting-edge technologies, deeper data insights, and enhanced services to transform how insurance is done. Visit our website at www.zinnia.com for more information. Apply by completing the online application on the careers section of our website. We are an Equal Opportunity employer committed to a diverse workforce. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability.

items.title: Senior Manager, Communications IMEA
items.description:
About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Manager, Communications to lead storytelling and communications for Tinder, Hinge, and The League across India and the Middle East. This is not a traditional PR role focused on press releases and media coverage alone, it is a cultural communications role designed to shape how our brands show up in conversations, communities, and culture.


You will be responsible for translating global and regional narratives into locally resonant stories that connect deeply with young audiences, particularly Gen Z and young millennials. You will operate at the intersection of earned media, social storytelling, and creator ecosystems to drive relevance, engagement, and brand meaning.

This role requires a strong operator who can both think strategically and execute end-to-end campaigns in fast-moving, culturally complex markets.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Manager, Communications to lead storytelling and communications for Tinder, Hinge, and The League across India and the Middle East. This is not a traditional PR role focused on press releases and media coverage alone, it is a cultural communications role designed to shape how our brands show up in conversations, communities, and culture.


You will be responsible for translating global and regional narratives into locally resonant stories that connect deeply with young audiences, particularly Gen Z and young millennials. You will operate at the intersection of earned media, social storytelling, and creator ecosystems to drive relevance, engagement, and brand meaning.

This role requires a strong operator who can both think strategically and execute end-to-end campaigns in fast-moving, culturally complex markets.

What You’ll Own

    1. Cultural Narrative & Local Resonance

    • Translate global and regional brand narratives into culturally relevant stories for India and Middle East markets

    • Identify cultural moments, trends, and tensions to position brands meaningfully in ongoing conversations

    • Ensure messaging is sensitive to regional nuances, including social norms, gender dynamics, and regulatory environments

      • 2. Product Communications

      • Lead communications for new feature launches across Tinder, Hinge, and The League

      • Translate product updates into compelling, user-facing stories that drive awareness and adoption

      • Partner closely with Product and Marketing teams to align on messaging, timing, and rollout strategies

        • 3. Consumer Communications (Earned)

        • Drive earned media through insight-led, value-based campaigns rather than transactional coverage

        • Build narratives that travel across media, creators, and cultural platforms

          • 4. Consumer Communications (Social-First Strategy)

          • Build and execute communications strategies that originate in social and digital ecosystems

          • Shape storytelling for platforms like Instagram, YouTube, Snapchat, and emerging channels

          • Partner with Marketing, Social, and Brand teams to ensure cohesive narratives across paid, owned, and earned channels

            • 5. Reputation & Issues Management

            • Lead proactive and reactive communications for sensitive topics and potential crises

            • Develop response frameworks suited for real-time, multi-platform environments (including X, Reddit, Instagram)

            • Partner with global communications, legal, and policy teams to manage risk

              • 6. Campaign Execution & Cross-Functional Leadership

              • Own end-to-end execution of communications campaigns across brands

              • Manage agency partners and ensure high-quality delivery

              • Collaborate closely with Marketing, Product, and Regional teams to align on priorities and timelines

                • 7. Measurement & Impact

                • Track performance across media, social, and cultural impact metrics

                • Measure success through narrative adoption, engagement, sentiment, and share of conversation

                • Connect communications efforts to broader brand and business outcomes

Who You Are

    • 8–12 years of experience in communications, PR, or brand storytelling

    • Strong experience beyond traditional PR, including social-first campaigns aknd creator ecosystems

    • Deep understanding of the India market; familiarity with Middle East markets is a strong plus

    • Proven ability to execute campaigns end-to-end in fast-paced environments

    • Strong instincts for internet culture, youth trends, and digital platforms

    • Experience managing sensitive topics, reputation issues, or crisis communications

    • Ability to work independently while collaborating across global and regional teams

What Success Looks Like

    • Our brands are meaningfully present in cultural conversations across India and the Middle East

    • Narratives originate in social ecosystems and extend into earned media

    • Strong network of creators and cultural partners driving ongoing relevance

    • Communications outcomes reflect depth, engagement, and impact not just volume of coverage

    • Improved brand perception among young audiences in key markets

Why This Role Matters

    This role is critical in shaping how Tinder, Hinge, and The League are understood and talked about in some of our most dynamic and complex markets. You will help define the cultural relevance of our brands in a way that goes far beyond traditional PR, building narratives that truly connect with how people live, date, and express themselves today.

#MG

items.title: Manager, Field Marketing
items.description:
About Zscaler

Zscaler accelerates digital transformation to ensure our customers can be more agile, efficient, resilient, and secure. As an AI-forward enterprise, we are constantly pushing the envelope, leveraging the world’s largest security data lake to power our cloud-native Zero Trust Exchange platform. This innovation protects our customers from cyberattacks and data loss by securely connecting users, devices, and applications in any location.

Here, impact in your role matters more than title and trust is built on results. We say, impact over activity. We seek innovators who actively use AI to amplify their impact and who thrive in an environment where we leverage intelligent systems to stay ahead of evolving threats. We believe in transparency and value constructive, honest debate—we’re focused on getting to the best ideas, faster. We build high-performing teams that can make an impact quickly and with high quality. To do this, we are building a culture of execution centered on customer obsession, collaboration, ownership, and accountability.

We value high-impact, high-accountability with a sense of urgency where you’re enabled to do your best work and embrace your potential. If you’re driven by purpose, thrive on solving complex challenges, and want to be part of the team that’s helping to secure the AI age, we invite you to bring your talents to Zscaler and help shape the future of cybersecurity.

Role 

We are looking for an experienced Manager, Field Marketing to join our team. This is a hybrid role based in Bangalore, India, reporting to the Director Marketing, India within the Marketing department. You’ll collaborate with diverse, creative people around the globe to hone the Zscaler brand, increase awareness and demand, and drive home big wins for the world’s cloud security leader and our customers worldwide.

What you’ll do (Role Expectations)

  • Develop marketing strategy in sync with local business and global priorities by partnering with sales, SDRs, and cross-functional teams
  • Run quarterly field marketing activities from awareness to demand generation to hit KPIs including pipe progression and marketing-sourced pipeline
  • Deploy always-on digital marketing for top-funnel coverage and run account-centric programs for deeper penetration into larger accounts
  • Manage the field marketing budget and co-op funds while driving measurable results against goals for MQLs and meetings
  • Partner with CoE teams to scale marketing execution in India across communications, customer advocacy, and CXO programs

Who You Are (Success Profile)

  • You thrive in ambiguity. You're comfortable building the path as you walk it. You thrive in a dynamic environment, seeing ambiguity not as a hindrance, but as the raw material to build something meaningful.
  • You act like an owner. Your passion for the mission fuels your bias for action. You operate with integrity because you genuinely care about the outcome. True ownership involves leveraging dynamic range: the ability to navigate seamlessly between high-level strategy and hands-on execution.
  • You are a problem-solver. You love running towards the challenges because you are laser-focused on finding the solution, knowing that solving the hard problems delivers the biggest impact.
  • You are a high-trust collaborator. You are ambitious for the team, not just yourself. You embrace our challenge culture by giving and receiving ongoing feedback—knowing that candor delivered with clarity and respect is the truest form of teamwork and the fastest way to earn trust.
  • You are a learner. You have a true growth mindset and are obsessed with your own development, actively seeking feedback to become a better partner and a stronger teammate. You love what you do and you do it with purpose.

What We’re Looking for (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 6–12 years of in-country field marketing experience across both direct and partner-led channels with proven results in pipeline growth
  • Demonstrated experience in digital and omni-channel marketing including ABM, social, SEM, and community marketing
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to derive actionable business insights from data to drive smart decision-making
  • Proficiency in Salesforce, Google Suite, and AI productivity tools

What Will Make You Stand Out (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing or Technology; MBA preferred
  • Previous experience working within a high-growth SaaS or cybersecurity vendor environment
  • Proven track record of utilizing AI tools for field marketing executions such as personalization, lead generation, and workflow automation

#LI-Hybrid 

#LI-SP4

At Zscaler, we are committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve and the customers we work with. We foster an inclusive environment that values all backgrounds and perspectives, emphasizing collaboration and belonging. Join us in our mission to make doing business seamless and secure.

Our Benefits program is one of the most important ways we support our employees. Zscaler proudly offers comprehensive and inclusive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families throughout their life stages, including:

  • Various health plans
  • Time off plans for vacation and sick time
  • Parental leave options
  • Retirement options
  • Education reimbursement
  • In-office perks, and more!

Learn more about Zscaler’s Future of Work strategy, hybrid working model, and benefits here.

By applying for this role, you adhere to applicable laws, regulations, and Zscaler policies, including those related to security and privacy standards and guidelines.

Zscaler is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all individuals. We strive to create a workplace where employees are treated with respect and have the chance to succeed. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy or related medical conditions), age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. See more information by clicking on the Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal link.

Pay Transparency

Zscaler complies with all applicable federal, state, and local pay transparency rules.

Zscaler is committed to providing reasonable support (called accommodations or adjustments) in our recruiting processes for candidates who are differently abled, have long term conditions, mental health conditions or sincerely held religious beliefs, or who are neurodivergent or require pregnancy-related support.

items.title: Influencer Marketing Manager
items.description:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Salary range: Rs 100000 - Rs 700000 (ie INR 1-7 LPA)

Min Experience: 3 years

Location: Mumbai

JobType: full-time

We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Influencer Marketing Manager to lead and scale our influencer partnerships and campaigns. This role is ideal for someone who understands the evolving creator economy and has hands-on experience in building, managing, and optimizing influencer marketing strategies across multiple platforms. You will play a key role in shaping brand perception, driving engagement, and delivering measurable ROI through impactful influencer collaborations.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop and execute end-to-end influencer marketing strategies aligned with brand goals, target audience, and campaign objectives.
  • Identify, evaluate, and onboard relevant influencers across platforms such as Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, and emerging channels.
  • Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with influencers, creators, and talent agencies.
  • Plan and manage influencer campaigns including product launches, brand awareness initiatives, and performance-driven collaborations.
  • Negotiate contracts, deliverables, timelines, and compensation structures with influencers while ensuring cost efficiency.
  • Collaborate closely with internal teams including marketing, content, and design to ensure consistent messaging and brand alignment.
  • Monitor campaign performance using KPIs such as reach, engagement, conversions, and ROI; generate insights and optimize future campaigns accordingly.
  • Stay updated with industry trends, competitor activities, and platform algorithm changes to keep strategies relevant and effective.
  • Ensure compliance with brand guidelines, legal requirements, and platform policies for influencer marketing.
  • Manage budgets and track expenditures for influencer campaigns.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

  • 3–7 years of proven experience in influencer marketing, digital marketing, or social media marketing.
  • Strong understanding of influencer ecosystems, content trends, and platform dynamics.
  • Demonstrated experience working directly with influencers and managing end-to-end influencer campaigns.
  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills with the ability to build authentic relationships with influencers.
  • Analytical mindset with proficiency in tracking campaign performance and deriving actionable insights.
  • Familiarity with influencer marketing tools, CRM platforms, and social media analytics tools.
  • Creative thinker with the ability to conceptualize engaging and innovative influencer-led campaigns.
  • Strong project management skills with attention to detail and ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working in fast-paced environments such as startups, agencies, or consumer brands.
  • Understanding of content creation, storytelling, and audience engagement strategies.
  • Exposure to performance marketing and conversion tracking is a plus.

items.title: Retail Marketing Manager
items.description:
Our mission at Oura is to empower every person to own their inner potential. Our award-winning products help our global community gain a deeper knowledge of their readiness, activity, and sleep quality by using their Oura Ring and its connected app. We've helped millions of people understand and improve their health by providing daily insights and practical steps to inspire healthy lifestyles.

Empowering the world starts with living our values and empowering our team. As a quickly growing company focused on helping people live healthier and happier lives, we ensure that our team members have what they need to do their best work — both in and out of the office. 

ŌURA is seeking a Retail Marketing Manager to lead the development, execution, and optimization of retail marketing programs across key retail and e-tail partners, including Target, Walmart, and specialty retailers domestically and globally. This role will manage retail marketing budgets, negotiate partner investments, develop merchandising plans, and collaborate cross-functionally to maximize Oura’s brand visibility, retail performance, and revenue across channels.

The ideal candidate is analytical, highly organized, an excellent communicator, and experienced in driving measurable business outcomes through channel marketing programs.

Preferred location is San Francisco or New York, remote candidates will be considered.

What You Will Do:

Retail Marketing Strategy & Planning

  • Manage annual retail marketing budgets including co-op dollars, MDF, and above the line spend , ensuring efficiency, accuracy, and alignment with channel goals and company revenue targets.
  • Track, measure, and optimize performance across all retail marketing investments to ensure ROI and continuous improvement.
  • Develop and execute in-channel marketing plans for major retailers and e-tailers in the U.S. and international markets.
  • Negotiate co-marketing investments, in-kind placements, and promotional opportunities with key accounts to maximize visibility and impact.

Online and In-store Merchandising & Retail Experience

  • Oversee in-channel online product listings (content, imagery, copy, and optimization) to ensure accuracy and maximize conversion.
  • Create and maintain plan-o-grams, merchandising guidelines, and in-store experience strategies for major retailers.
  • Collaborate with internal and external partners to secure optimal display space, end caps, feature placement, and e-commerce visibility.

Revenue & Promotion Management

  • Identify and coordinate around key drive times (e.g., seasonal moments, product launches, retailer events) to maximize marketing placements and brand exposure.
  • Manage development, implementation, and optimization of direct and channel-based promotions, ensuring programs meet growth objectives and adhere to brand standards.

Channel Partner Relationship Management

  • Serve as day-to-day contact for retail and e-tail partners, ensuring productive collaboration, timely communication, and seamless execution of joint initiatives.
  • Work closely with Sales, Operations, Brand, Creative, and Product teams to deliver integrated and channel-appropriate marketing initiatives.

Analytics, Reporting & Insights

  • Leverage business analytics, retail data, and shopper insights to evaluate program effectiveness and drive data-backed decision-making.
  • Build dashboards, reports, and presentations that clearly communicate performance, learnings, and future recommendations to leadership and cross-functional stakeholders.
  • Continuously refine strategies and tactics based on insights, trends, and competitive analysis.

Requirements
We would love to consider you for this role if you have:

  • 5–7 years of experience in retail marketing, channel marketing, trade marketing, or shopper marketing within consumer goods, electronics, wearables, or related industries.
  • Experience managing retail marketing budgets and optimizing spend for measurable results.
  • Familiarity with major U.S. retailers (Target, Walmart, Best Buy, Costco, etc.) and/or international retailers and marketplaces.
  • Strong negotiation skills and experience securing co-marketing investments or added-value placements.
  • Understanding of merchandising strategy, plan-o-gram development, and e-commerce content best practices.
  • Excellent analytical skills; ability to translate data into actionable insights and strategic recommendations.
  • Strong cross-functional collaboration and communication skills; ability to influence and align across teams and external partners.
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Proficiency with analytics tools (e.g., Looker, Tableau), retail portals, and project management software a plus.

Benefits
At Oura, we care about you and your well-being. Everyone here at Oura has a ring of their own and we are continually looking to improve employee health.

What we offer:

  • Competitive salary and equity packages
  • Health, dental, vision insurance, and mental health resources
  • An Oura Ring of your own plus employee discounts for friends & family
  • 20 days of paid time off plus 13 paid holidays plus 8 days of flexible wellness time off
  • Paid sick leave and parental leave

Oura takes a market-based approach to pay, which may vary depending on your location. US locations are categorized into tiers based on a cost of labor index for that geographic area. While most offers will be closer to the starting range, successful candidates' pay will be determined based on job-related skills, experience, qualifications, work location, internal peer equity, and market conditions. These ranges may be modified in the future.

  • Region 1 $119,000 - $140,000
  • Region 2 $106,250 - $125,000
  • Region 3 $94,450 - $117,000

A recruiter can determine your zones/tiers based on your US location.

We are not considering candidates residing in the following states: Alaska (AK), Delaware (DE), Iowa (IA), Mississippi (MS), Nebraska (NE), South Dakota (SD), West Virginia (WV), and Wisconsin (WI)

Oura is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Individuals seeking employment at Oura are considered without regard to age, ancestry, color, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, protected family care or medical leave status, race, religion (including beliefs and practices or the absence thereof), sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. We will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics.

We will work to ensure individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment.

Disclaimer: Beware of fake job offers!
We’ve been alerted to scammers posing as ŌURA recruiters, especially for remote roles. Please note:

  • Our jobs are listed only on the ŌURA Careers page and trusted job boards.
  • We will never ask for personal information like ID or payment for equipment upfront.
  • Official offers are sent through Docusign after a verbal offer, not via text or email.

Stay cautious and protect your personal details.

To all recruitment agencies: Oura does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Oura employees, or any other organization's location. Oura is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

items.title: Creator Marketing Manager
items.description:
Established in 2004, OLIVER is the world’s first and only specialist in designing, building, and running bespoke in-house agencies and marketing ecosystems for brands. We partner with over 300 clients in 40+ countries and counting. Our unique model drives creativity and efficiency, allowing us to deliver tailored solutions that resonate deeply with audiences. 

As a part of The Brandtech Group, we're at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge AI technology to revolutionise how we create and deliver work. Our AI solutions enhance efficiency, spark creativity, and drive insightful decision-making, empowering our teams to produce innovative and impactful results. 

Role:  Creator Marketing Manager

Location:  Gurgaon, India

 

About the role: 

As the Creator Marketing Manager for YouTube Shorts, you will be the driving force behind how we engage, recruit, and amplify the next generation of digital storytellers in India. This is a high-ownership role that sits at the intersection of brand strategy, data-driven experimentation, and cultural trend-spotting. You will lead the Go-To-Market (GTM) strategy, managing multi-million dollar campaigns and building a robust ecosystem of creators, labels, and partners.

What you will be doing: 

1. Strategy & GTM Leadership

● Own the Mandate: Lead the end-to-end GTM strategy for YouTube Shorts in India,
overseeing influencer campaign management from briefing to execution.

● Partner Ecosystem: Orchestrate collaboration between creative agencies, media
partners, talent labels, and internal business teams to drive product adoption.

● Paid Media Integration: Partner with media agencies to implement high-impact paid
strategies that scale reach and engagement.

2. Creator Recruitment & Optimization

● Growth Engines: Develop and execute scalable strategies to recruit and optimize
creator participation across YouTube products (Shorts, Shopping, Music).

● Brand Stewardship: Ensure all agency-led executions align with global brand
guidelines while maintaining local cultural relevance.

● Trend Monitoring: Act as the internal "pulse" for the creator economy, identifying
emerging trends and mitigating brand risks in real-time.

3. Cross-Functional Collaboration

● Global Alignment: Work seamlessly with APAC Marketing and Global Social teams
to localize product launches and share best practices.
● Stakeholder Management: Present campaign performance and strategic roadmaps
to senior leadership with clarity and data-backed confidence.

4. Experimentation & Analysis

● Test & Learn: Design and run robust marketing experiments to optimize creator
selection and creative formats.

● Data Synthesis: Translate complex campaign analytics into actionable insights,
socializing "wins and fails" across the organization to inform future strategy.

 

What you need to be great in this role: 

  • Experience: 9+ years in Marketing or Strategy (Agency or Client-side). A track
    record in the social media or creator ecosystem is a plus, but not a prerequisite for a
    fast learner.
  • Extreme Ownership: You are a self-starter who can navigate ambiguity and build a
    point of view from scratch.
  • Analytical Intuition: You are comfortable with data and social analytics tools, but
    you also possess the "creative gut" to know what content will go viral.
  • Project Mastery: Proven ability to manage complex budgets and multiple
    high-stakes campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
  • Communication: Exceptional written and verbal skills; you can tell a compelling
    story to an audience of creators or a boardroom of executives.

Why You’ll Love This Role

You will have the autonomy to shape the future of short-form video in one of the world’s most dynamic markets. If you are a proactive problem-solver who thrives on experimentation and has a deep passion for the creator economy, this is your stage.

Our values shape everything we do: 

Be Ambitious to succeed   

Be Imaginative to push the boundaries of what’s possible   

Be Inspirational to do groundbreaking work   

Be always learning and listening to understand   

Be Results-focused  to exceed expectations   

Be actively pro-inclusive and anti-racist across our community, clients and creations   

OLIVER, a part of the Brandtech Group, is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive working environment where all employees are encouraged to reach their full potential, and individual differences are valued and respected. All applicants shall be considered for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, neurodivergence, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by local laws.    

 OLIVER has set ambitious environmental goals around sustainability, with science-based emissions reduction targets. Collectively, we work towards our mission, embedding sustainability into every department and through every stage of the project lifecycle.

items.title: Senior Marketing Manager
items.description:
Established in 2004, OLIVER is the world’s first and only specialist in designing, building, and running bespoke in-house agencies and marketing ecosystems for brands. We partner with over 300 clients in 40+ countries and counting. Our unique model drives creativity and efficiency, allowing us to deliver tailored solutions that resonate deeply with audiences. 

As a part of The Brandtech Group, we're at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge AI technology to revolutionise how we create and deliver work. Our AI solutions enhance efficiency, spark creativity, and drive insightful decision-making, empowering our teams to produce innovative and impactful results. 

Role:  Brand Integration & Content Manager - Strategic Alliances CoE

Location:  Gurgaon, India

 

About the role: 

We are setting up a Strategic Alliances Centre of Excellence (CoE) to shift Google India Marketing from transactional, media-led deals to long-term, value-multiplying alliances across key products and strategically important business themes.

The Brand Integration & Content Manager sits at the intersection of brand, content, and commercial partnerships. Serving as the connective tissue between Google and the Partner Organization, you will own the delivery of Tier 1 partnerships with top-tier creators, public figures, major broadcast networks, and global brands. This role demands a leader who is equally fluent in brand strategy and editorial thinking, ensuring every co-branded initiative, partner campaign, and collaborative content piece is executed with creative integrity, strategic coherence, and measurable impact.

 

What you will be doing: 

1. High-Stakes Partnership Management

Serve as the primary lead for tier 1 partnerships, managing complex relationships with top-tier creators, public
figures, major broadcast networks, and leading brands.

Translate high-level agreements into detailed execution plans: milestones, timelines, workstreams, and owners.

Drive day-to-day progress to ensure all agreed deliverables are achieved in full and on time.

2. Brand Integration, Stewardship & Advocacy

Fiercely protect Google’s brand equity during all phases of the partnership to ensure the company’s reputation and
values are upheld.

Ensure Google’s products show up in the best possible way across all partner assets and experiences.

Develop and maintain a Brand Integration Playbook, acting as the primary brand counsel to partnership managers
during deal structuring.

Work with brand and creative teams to enforce brand guardrails, visual identity, tone of voice, and policy guidelines,
proactively flagging risk issues before they impact the public.

3. Content Strategy & Production

Build and execute a content calendar specifically for partnerships, translating commercial relationships into
compelling stories across owned, earned, and partner channels.

Develop content formats tailored to different partnership types (e.g., co-authored thought leadership,
behind-the-scenes integrations, joint research reports).

Write, edit, and commission long-form and short-form content, working tightly with internal creative teams and
external agencies to produce high-quality assets at pace.

4. Single Point of Contact (Internal & External)

Act as the single point of contact for the partner for all operational, content, and execution matters.

Manage creative briefs, feedback loops, and approval workflows between external partners and internal teams.

Coordinate internally with Product Marketing, Brand, Creative, Media, PR/Comms, and Measurement teams.

Ensure information flows smoothly between the partner and internal teams, avoiding duplication and misalignment.

5. Measurement, Risk & Optimisation

Define KPIs for partner content programs (engagement, reach, brand perception lift) and ensure tracking is set up
before launch.

Identify execution risks early (delays, scope creep, creative issues) and escalate in a structured way with actionable
recommendations.

Conduct content audits and post-campaign reviews to surface learnings, highlight impact vs. objectives, and
recommend strategies for scaling.

 

What you need to be great in this role: 

  • Experience: 8-10 years of experience in brand management, content strategy, or integrated marketing, ideally in tech,
    media, or entertainment.
  • Track Record: Proven experience managing high-profile external partners, such as major networks,
    celebrities/creators, or top-tier brands, taking campaigns from deal signing to post-mortem.
  • Craft: Exceptional writing and editorial skills, with the ability to adapt brand voice and format across channels and
    audiences.
  • Brand Acumen: Strong understanding of brand architecture, visual identity systems, and co-branding principles.
  • Execution: Excellent project management skills, with a demonstrated ability to juggle multiple workstreams, manage
    creative workflows, and hit tight deadlines.
  • Experience managing large-scale, complex partnerships (e.g., OTT, sports leagues, or national broadcast networks).
  • Familiarity with content management systems, Digital Asset Management (DAM) tools, and creative project
    management platforms (e.g., Figma, Asana).
  • Experience working in or with cross-country/regional teams.
  • Exposure to performance marketing metrics and an understanding of how content feeds into brand health.
  • Dual Fluency: You speak both "brand" and "editorial" natively.
  • Operator Mindset: You enjoy making things happen, closing loops, and sweating the details.
  • Diplomat: You have a sharp eye for brand integrity and possess the proven ability to influence without authority in
    external rooms.
  • Problem-Solver: You anticipate issues, surface them early, and always come with solutions.
