items.title: Principal Product Marketing Manager-Platform & Ecosystem
items.description:
Who We Are

Simpplr is the AI-powered intranet for unifying the digital workplace. It brings people, trusted knowledge, apps, and agents into a coherent digital experience. Powered by a proprietary EX Knowledge Graph, Simpplr synthesizes signals and context across connected systems to deliver personalized information and actions. The platform serves as a digital hub supporting communications, engagement, employee services, and work. With low-code extensibility and enterprise-grade security and governance, Simpplr enables confident operation at scale. More than 1,000 organizations — including AAA, the NHS, Penske, and Moderna — trust Simpplr to keep their workforce informed, aligned, and productive. Learn more at simpplr.com.

The Opportunity

Simpplr is a recognized leader in the digital workplace and employee experience space, with a rapidly expanding product portfolio and a vision to redefine how work happens. As we continue to scale, we’re hiring a Principal Product Marketing Manager to own Simpplr’s platform foundations and ecosystem, spanning the capabilities and services that enable enterprise customers to deploy, extend, and realize value from the platform at scale.

This role sits at the intersection of enterprise platform readiness and customer value realization, defining how Simpplr is deployed, governed, integrated, extended, and successfully operationalized across complex organizations. This seasoned PMM will elevate how Simpplr tells its story around enterprise platform services, extensibility, interoperability, ecosystem value, and professional services.

Reporting to the Director of Product Marketing, this role partners closely with Product, Partnerships, Services, Sales, and Enablement teams to craft powerful narratives and support launches and GTM programs.

Your Responsibilities

What you will be doing:

Enterprise Platform Foundations and Experience

  • Own the positioning, messaging, and storytelling for Simpplr’s horizontal platform services and experience (e.g., user provisioning, audiences and segmentation, access controls, accessibility).
  • Articulate how these foundational capabilities enable secure, personalized, scalable, and compliant employee experiences across the enterprise.
  • Develop value narratives that resonate with IT, security, and architecture stakeholders while clearly connecting platform services to usability, adoption, and business outcomes.
  • Position these capabilities as the foundation that enables enterprise adoption, extensibility, AI-driven experiences, and long-term platform scalability.

Integration and Extensibility

  • Own the positioning, messaging, and storytelling around Simpplr’s integrations and extensibility capabilities (e.g., pre-built integrations, app tiles, extensibility center, APIs).
  • Articulate how Simpplr connects with enterprise systems across HR, IT, collaboration, content, and line-of-business applications.
  • Develop differentiated stories that explain how customers extend, automate, and orchestrate work on Simpplr without heavy customization.

Partner and Ecosystem Marketing

  • Build and scale GTM narratives for Simpplr’s partner ecosystem, including technology partners, ISVs, and strategic alliances.
  • Collaborate with the Partnerships team to develop joint value propositions, partner playbooks, co-marketing assets, and field enablement resources.
  • Position Simpplr’s ecosystem as a force multiplier for platform value, extensibility, and customer outcomes.

Services and Customer Outcomes

  • Develop positioning and sales assets for Simpplr’s professional services, accelerators, packaged offerings, and implementation frameworks.
  • Partner with the Services organization to articulate ROI, customer impact, and the role services play in successful deployments.
  • Position professional services as a core lever for enterprise readiness and value realization, not just implementation.
  • Create customer-facing content that showcases methodology, best practices, and differentiated service delivery.

Go-to-Market Leadership

  • Support launch planning and execution across integrations, ecosystem partnerships, and services.
  • Create high-impact enablement resources including decks, one-pagers, battlecards, demos, and RFP-ready narratives.
  • Partner with Product to influence roadmap alignment with customer needs, partner requirements, and competitive trends.

Competitive and Analyst Support

  • Maintain a clear POV on differentiation across platform services, extensibility, ecosystem, and services.
  • Collaborate with Analyst Relations to ensure Simpplr’s platform and ecosystem story is accurately represented in key evaluations and reports.

Your Skillset

What makes you a great fit for the team:

  • 6+ years of product marketing experience in B2B SaaS or enterprise software, ideally with experience in platform services and/or integrations and extensibility frameworks.
  • Ability to translate complex technical and platform concepts into compelling, business-relevant value stories.
  • Experience positioning products for enterprise IT, security, and architecture stakeholders, and connecting platform readiness to business outcomes.
  • Experience partnering with Product, Partnerships, Services, and Sales to shape roadmap priorities, drive adoption, and support enterprise opportunities.
  • Strong understanding of enterprise sales cycles, RFP processes, and the needs of both business buyers and IT evaluators.
  • Proven ability to build high-quality technical and sales enablement content that supports product-led and partner-led deals.
  • Exceptional storytelling and communication skills, with the ability to connect platform-level capabilities to concrete customer outcomes.
  • Systems thinker who can connect platform foundations, extensibility, ecosystem, and services to Simpplr’s broader platform and AI story.
  • Curiosity and adaptability to quickly develop subject-matter expertise in areas such as identity, access control, APIs, enterprise integrations, and implementation methodology.

Benefits:

Simpplr provides a competitive compensation package along with full health, vision, and dental benefits. We believe in work-life integration and offer a flexible work environment. These benefits, coupled with an amazing team of individuals who believe in our mission and value openness, collaboration and teamwork, make Simpplr an incredible place to work.

 



Your Voice, Unfiltered:

We value the real you. To ensure a fair and authentic experience for everyone, we ask that you do not use AI tools (such as real-time answer generators, transcription apps, or note-taking bots) during your interview

Our process is designed to hear your unique story, thought process, and lived experience in real-time. Use of unauthorized AI tools may result in disqualification, as we want to ensure every candidate is evaluated on their own individual merits. We’re excited to meet the person behind the resume!

If you need assistive technology or AI tools for accessibility (e.g., live captioning), please notify your recruiter in advance. We are committed to providing an inclusive interview experience.

Simpplr’s Hub-Hybrid-Remote Model:

At Simpplr we believe that when work is good, life is better and that belief guides all we do. Including how we approach our flexible work model. Simpplr operates with a Hub-Hybrid-Remote model. This model is role-based with exceptions and provides employees with the flexibility that many have told us they want.

  • Hub - 100% work from Simpplr office. Role requires Simpplifier to be in the office full-time.
  • Hybrid - Hybrid work from home and office. Role dictates the ability to work from home, plus benefit from in-person collaboration on a regular basis. 
  • Remote - 100% remote. Role can be done anywhere within your country of hire, as long as the requirements of the role are met.

items.title: Product Strategy & Marketing Manager (Partnerships)
items.description:
About Us

We are a global learning platform on a mission to motivate every learner by empowering every educator.

From K–12 classrooms and universities to corporate training settings, our platform is trusted in over 150+ countries by 75 million monthly active users. In the U.S., 90% of schools use Wayground to engage students and transform learning through technology.

Wayground is a high-growth, profitable SaaS company backed by leading investors including Tiger Global, GSV, and Nexus Venture Partners.

If you're excited about education and international SaaS and want to build towards a mission that you can be proud of, then Wayground is the right place for you.

About the Role

We are looking for a strategic, creative, and highly resourceful Product Strategy & Marketing Manager (Partnerships) to help shape how our partners position, package, and bring Wayground to market.

This role sits at the intersection of product strategy, solution consulting, product marketing, partner enablement, and partner growth. You will work closely with Partner Managers and partners to support strategic opportunities, build scalable partner-facing programs and resources, and help design the frameworks that enable partners to succeed.

This is not a traditional channel operations role — it is a strategic, builder-oriented role for someone who wants to influence how partnerships go to market and scale.

What you'll do:

    1. Drive Solution Strategy for Partner Opportunities

  • Partner closely with Partner Managers to support strategic customer opportunities across schools, ministries, publishers, and institutions.

  • Understand partner and customer requirements and translate them into tailored solutions that map Wayground’s capabilities to real use cases.

  • Support high-impact opportunities including tenders, pilots, proposals, demos, and customized solution presentations.

  • Package repeatable solution offerings and value propositions around priority use cases, segments, and markets.

  • Serve as a strategic thought partner in shaping how Wayground is positioned in complex partner-led opportunities.

    •  

    2. Build Partner Enablement & Readiness Programs

  • Design scalable frameworks for partner onboarding, training, and readiness that improve how partners ramp, position, and succeed with Wayground.

  • Develop partner training journeys, certifications, onboarding playbooks, and capability-building programs, working with supporting team members on execution.

  • Build partner enablement resource kits including presentations, solution briefs, pitch decks, flyers, landing pages, sales tools, and playbooks.

  • Craft messaging and positioning resources that help partners effectively communicate Wayground’s value to end customers.

  • Create repeatable frameworks, assets, and tools that equip partners to sell, support, and grow successfully.

  • Identify partner capability gaps and design programs or resources to address them.

  • Use AI and modern tools to rapidly create and iterate high-quality enablement and go-to-market assets.

    • 3. Shape Partner Strategy & Growth Programs

  • Help design partner growth plans and go-to-market motions that support demand generation and pipeline growth.

  • Develop scalable partner growth programs such as campaigns, webinars, workshops, and activation plays.

  • Support the design of strategic partner programs, including tiering models, engagement programs, incentives, and frameworks that help drive stronger partner performance.

  • Identify repeatable growth motions and strategic plays that can scale across partners and regions.

  • Work with Partner Managers to identify opportunities to deepen partner engagement, productivity, and revenue contribution.

    •  

    4. Build Scalable Frameworks and Support Execution

  • Create repeatable templates, frameworks, and playbooks that improve consistency and speed across partner-facing work.

  • Help improve systems and processes that support a scalable partnerships business.

  • Provide guidance to a small execution/operations team supporting partner and community programs.

  • Work cross-functionally with partnerships, product, sales, and marketing teams to bring initiatives to life.

Who you are:

  • 5–8 years of experience in product marketing, solutions consulting, strategic partnerships, partner enablement, growth strategy, or related roles in SaaS, EdTech, or B2B environments.

  • Strong product intuition and ability to translate product capabilities into compelling partner and customer solutions.

  • Experience building enablement frameworks, partner-facing programs, or go-to-market initiatives.

  • Excellent storytelling, messaging, and presentation skills.

  • Strategic thinker with strong problem-solving instincts and comfort operating in ambiguity.

  • Ability to create structure and scalable frameworks from scratch.

  • High ownership mindset with a bias for action.

  • Comfortable using AI and modern tools to move fast and build creatively.

  • Experience in education technology, channel ecosystems, or partner-led growth is a plus.

Why This Role is Unique

This role combines product strategy, product marketing, solution consulting, partner enablement, and growth strategy in one highly entrepreneurial function.

You will help shape not just how partners are supported — but how they go to market, how opportunities are won, and how partnership growth scales.


Working at Wayground

We're a small, passionate, tech-first team solving big challenges in education. We move fast, build with data, and obsess over creating delightful experiences for our global community of educators and learners.

The pandemic has changed work in many ways, especially by enabling remote work. Many companies have chosen to be remote first. At Wayground, we work best in the office brainstorming using whiteboards, having conversations over coffee/walks, or overhearing a hallway conversation. Learning happens through osmosis. We understand that driving/ubering takes work in Bangalore. We’ll take care of the commute for you.

Beyond taking care of commute, we will cover health care benefits for you, your family, and your parents. We invest in our employee’s health and wellness. Through books and courses, we invest in their learning and development. Oh, and finally, we have healthy lunches, dinners, and a stocked pantry to snack on all day.

Wayground is an equal opportunity employer and we believe diversity is key to our success.

items.title: Product Marketing Manager (Engagement & Retention)
items.description:
About Fam (previously FamPay)

Fam is India's first payments app for everyone above 11. FamApp helps make online and offline payments through UPI and FamCard. We are on a mission to raise a new, financially aware generation, and drive 250 million+ youngest users in India to kickstart their financial journey super early in their life. Founded in 2019 by IIT Roorkee alumni, Fam is backed by some of the most respected investors around the world like Elevation Capital, Y-Combinator, Peak XV (Sequoia Capital) India, Venture Highway, Global Founder’s Capital and the likes of Kunal Shah, Amrish Rao as angel investors.
 
Role Overview

We’re looking for a Product Marketing Manager to build and scale Fam’s lifecycle and engagement engine.

This is a 1→10 role — you’ll own CRM end-to-end, drive user activation and retention, and turn engagement channels into a consistent, high-impact growth lever.

This role sits at the intersection of product, growth, and user behavior — requiring strong ownership of lifecycle journeys, sharp experimentation, and a deep understanding of what drives repeat usage.

Must-haves

  • 5–7 yrs in B2C product marketing / CRM / lifecycle marketing
  • Hands-on experience with CleverTap / MoEngage / WebEngage (or similar)
  • Strong understanding of engagement metrics (CTR, open rates, conversion, retention)
  • Experience owning push, WhatsApp, email channels end-to-end
  • Proven ability to drive retention and repeat usage
  • Strong prioritisation — can manage asks across multiple PMs
  • Data-driven + experimentation mindset
  • Should be AI native
  • High ownership; moves fast

Good to haves

 • Gen Z audience understanding
 • Exposure to personalisation, segmentation, lifecycle journeys
 • Basic copy + messaging instincts for high CTRs
 • Experience setting up CRM systems from scratch and scaling them 

Roles and Responsibilities

1. Lifecycle & Engagement (Own)

  • Own push, WhatsApp, email across the user lifecycle
  • Drive activation, retention, and repeat transactions
  • Build structured user journeys (onboarding → habit → reactivation)

2. CRM Platform Ownership (Own)

  • Own CleverTap (or equivalent) end-to-end
  • Set up segmentation, cohorts, triggers, and journeys
  • Ensure hygiene, tracking, and scalability of CRM systems

3. Centralisation & Governance (Own)

  • Move from PM-led fragmented sends → centralised CRM function
  • Define who sends what, when, and to whom
  • Build shared learnings, playbooks, and best practices

4. Experimentation (Own)

  • Run high-volume tests on copy, timing, frequency, segmentation
  • Optimize for CTR, conversion, and downstream actions
  • Build a strong experimentation cadence

5. Cross-functional Collaboration (Drive)

  • Work with multiple PMs to prioritise campaigns
  • Balance competing asks with business impact
  • Influence product decisions through user insights

What Success Looks Like
  • Improved CTR, engagement, and retention metrics
  • Clear, centralised CRM strategy (vs fragmented sends)
  • Higher repeat transactions per user
  • Strong experimentation velocity and learnings
  • CRM becomes a key growth lever, not just a support channel

Why Join Us
  • Opportunity to build a category-defining brand for the next generation in India
  • Work directly with founders and leadership
  • Own a high-impact growth lever from day one
  • Fast-paced, high-ownership culture
About Fam (previously FamPay)

Fam is India's first payments app for everyone above 11. FamApp helps make online and offline payments through UPI and FamCard. We are on a mission to raise a new, financially aware generation, and drive 250 million+ youngest users in India to kickstart their financial journey super early in their life. Founded in 2019 by IIT Roorkee alumni, Fam is backed by some of the most respected investors around the world like Elevation Capital, Y-Combinator, Peak XV (Sequoia Capital) India, Venture Highway, Global Founder’s Capital and the likes of Kunal Shah, Amrish Rao as angel investors.
 
Role Overview

We’re looking for a Product Marketing Manager to build and scale Fam’s lifecycle and engagement engine.

This is a 1→10 role — you’ll own CRM end-to-end, drive user activation and retention, and turn engagement channels into a consistent, high-impact growth lever.

This role sits at the intersection of product, growth, and user behavior — requiring strong ownership of lifecycle journeys, sharp experimentation, and a deep understanding of what drives repeat usage.

Must-haves

  • 5–7 yrs in B2C product marketing / CRM / lifecycle marketing
  • Hands-on experience with CleverTap / MoEngage / WebEngage (or similar)
  • Strong understanding of engagement metrics (CTR, open rates, conversion, retention)
  • Experience owning push, WhatsApp, email channels end-to-end
  • Proven ability to drive retention and repeat usage
  • Strong prioritisation — can manage asks across multiple PMs
  • Data-driven + experimentation mindset
  • Should be AI native
  • High ownership; moves fast

Good to haves

 • Gen Z audience understanding
 • Exposure to personalisation, segmentation, lifecycle journeys
 • Basic copy + messaging instincts for high CTRs
 • Experience setting up CRM systems from scratch and scaling them 

Roles and Responsibilities

1. Lifecycle & Engagement (Own)

  • Own push, WhatsApp, email across the user lifecycle
  • Drive activation, retention, and repeat transactions
  • Build structured user journeys (onboarding → habit → reactivation)

2. CRM Platform Ownership (Own)

  • Own CleverTap (or equivalent) end-to-end
  • Set up segmentation, cohorts, triggers, and journeys
  • Ensure hygiene, tracking, and scalability of CRM systems

3. Centralisation & Governance (Own)

  • Move from PM-led fragmented sends → centralised CRM function
  • Define who sends what, when, and to whom
  • Build shared learnings, playbooks, and best practices

4. Experimentation (Own)

  • Run high-volume tests on copy, timing, frequency, segmentation
  • Optimize for CTR, conversion, and downstream actions
  • Build a strong experimentation cadence

5. Cross-functional Collaboration (Drive)

  • Work with multiple PMs to prioritise campaigns
  • Balance competing asks with business impact
  • Influence product decisions through user insights

What Success Looks Like
  • Improved CTR, engagement, and retention metrics
  • Clear, centralised CRM strategy (vs fragmented sends)
  • Higher repeat transactions per user
  • Strong experimentation velocity and learnings
  • CRM becomes a key growth lever, not just a support channel

Why Join Us
  • Opportunity to build a category-defining brand for the next generation in India
  • Work directly with founders and leadership
  • Own a high-impact growth lever from day one
  • Fast-paced, high-ownership culture

items.title: Assistant Manager - Creative Communications (Design and Art), Telugu
items.description:

We serve high quality content to millions of customers across millions of devices, all of which are constantly evolving and changing. This role will lead the Graphics team.

The Creative Communications team is responsible for making and executing the channel’s overall communication strategy for the Channels. The team collaborates closely with the marketing, fiction, and programming teams to create engaging content promos, show trailers, and brand campaigns for the channel. With a deep understanding of the regional audience, the team creates impactful narratives that vibrate with viewers while supporting the channel’s growth and visibility. The team comprises promo producers, writers, graphic designers, and promo editors.
Job Summary:

items.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager- Employee Engagement & Frontline
items.description:
Who We Are

Simpplr is the AI-powered intranet for unifying the digital workplace. It brings people, trusted knowledge, apps, and agents into a coherent digital experience. Powered by a proprietary EX Knowledge Graph, Simpplr synthesizes signals and context across connected systems to deliver personalized information and actions. The platform serves as a digital hub supporting communications, engagement, employee services, and work. With low-code extensibility and enterprise-grade security and governance, Simpplr enables confident operation at scale. More than 1,000 organizations — including AAA, the NHS, Penske, and Moderna — trust Simpplr to keep their workforce informed, aligned, and productive. Learn more at simpplr.com.

The Opportunity

Simpplr is a recognized leader in the digital workplace and employee experience space, with a rapidly expanding product portfolio and a vision to redefine how work happens. As we continue to scale, we’re hiring a Senior Product Marketing Manager to own Simpplr’s employee voice, recognition, and frontline engagement experiences, driving insight, connection, and action across the workforce.

This role is responsible for the end-to-end go-to-market strategy and execution for these portfolio areas, including new innovations, feature evolution, and customer adoption. You will play a central role in shaping how these capabilities are brought to market, how they support customer outcomes, and how they contribute to Simpplr’s broader platform strategy.

Reporting to the Director of Product Marketing, this role partners closely with Product, Sales, Enablement, and Customer teams to drive successful launches and sustained market impact.

Your Responsibilities

What you will be doing:

Portfolio Ownership and Go-to-Market Leadership

  • Own the end-to-end go-to-market strategy and execution for Simpplr’s employee voice, recognition, and frontline engagement portfolios.
  • Lead planning and execution for new innovations and major enhancements, from early product shaping through launch, enablement, and adoption.
  • Partner with Product to influence roadmap priorities and ensure market readiness for upcoming releases.
  • Develop and maintain the core enablement assets required to support selling, expansion, and competitive differentiation, including decks, one-pagers, demos, battlecards, and RFP-ready narratives.
  • Partner with Sales, Enablement, and Customer teams to support active opportunities, frontline-heavy deployments, and expansion motions within your portfolio.

Cross-Functional and Market Leadership

  • Serve as the primary product marketing point of contact for your portfolio across Product, Sales, Enablement, Customer Success, and Analyst Relations.
  • Maintain an up-to-date understanding of customer needs, market dynamics, and competitive activity across employee engagement, listening, recognition, and frontline solutions.
  • Support analyst briefings, evaluations, and inquiries related to your areas of ownership.

Your Skillset

What makes you a great fit for the team:

  • 4+ years of product marketing experience in B2B SaaS or enterprise software.
  • Demonstrated experience owning go-to-market execution for products or portfolios, including launches, enablement, and adoption.
  • Strong ability to partner cross-functionally with Product, Sales, Enablement, and Customer teams.
  • Experience supporting enterprise sales cycles and complex buying environments.
  • Proven ability to create effective enablement assets that drive understanding and impact in the field.
  • Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution across multiple initiatives.
  • Experience or strong interest in employee engagement, recognition, HR technology, or workforce platforms.
  • Familiarity with selling to HR, Internal Communications, Operations, or frontline leadership audiences.
  • Experience supporting or marketing to frontline-heavy enterprise organizations is a plus.

Benefits:

Simpplr provides a competitive compensation package along with full health, vision, and dental benefits. We believe in work-life integration and offer a flexible work environment. These benefits, coupled with an amazing team of individuals who believe in our mission and value openness, collaboration and teamwork, make Simpplr an incredible place to work.

 



Your Voice, Unfiltered:

We value the real you. To ensure a fair and authentic experience for everyone, we ask that you do not use AI tools (such as real-time answer generators, transcription apps, or note-taking bots) during your interview

Our process is designed to hear your unique story, thought process, and lived experience in real-time. Use of unauthorized AI tools may result in disqualification, as we want to ensure every candidate is evaluated on their own individual merits. We’re excited to meet the person behind the resume!

If you need assistive technology or AI tools for accessibility (e.g., live captioning), please notify your recruiter in advance. We are committed to providing an inclusive interview experience.

Simpplr’s Hub-Hybrid-Remote Model:

At Simpplr we believe that when work is good, life is better and that belief guides all we do. Including how we approach our flexible work model. Simpplr operates with a Hub-Hybrid-Remote model. This model is role-based with exceptions and provides employees with the flexibility that many have told us they want.

  • Hub - 100% work from Simpplr office. Role requires Simpplifier to be in the office full-time.
  • Hybrid - Hybrid work from home and office. Role dictates the ability to work from home, plus benefit from in-person collaboration on a regular basis. 
  • Remote - 100% remote. Role can be done anywhere within your country of hire, as long as the requirements of the role are met.

items.title: Senior Field Marketing Manager - India (R4772)
items.description:
Founded in 2015, Shield AI is a venture-backed deep-tech company with the mission of protecting service members and civilians with intelligent systems. Its products include the V-BAT and X-BAT aircraft, Hivemind Enterprise, and the Hivemind Vision product lines. With offices and facilities across the U.S., Europe, the Middle East, and the Asia-Pacific, Shield AI’s technology actively supports operations worldwide. For more information, visit www.shield.ai. Follow Shield AI on LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and YouTube. 

Job Description:

Shield AI is building the world’s best AI pilot. As a fast-scaling defense aerospace company, we develop autonomous systems that operate in complex, contested environments to protect service members and civilians. Our mission sits at the intersection of artificial intelligence, advanced hardware, and national security. We are expanding our international go-to-market footprint and investing in strategic growth markets, including India.

The Senior Field Marketing Manager, India will establish and execute field marketing across India’s defense and government ecosystem. This leader will drive account-based marketing programs, support strategic capture efforts, and generate measurable pipeline impact in close partnership with regional sales and business development teams.

This is a hands-on role in a high-growth environment. The ideal candidate is equally comfortable developing account strategies and executing field programs, thrives in complex government sales environments, and understands how to align marketing efforts with long-cycle defense procurement processes. This role reports to the VP of Global Field Marketing and Events.

What you'll do:

  • Lead and execute field marketing across India defense, Ministry of Defence, Armed Forces, DRDO, and strategic aerospace accounts, tightly aligned with regional Go-to-Market priorities.
  • Develop and execute account-based marketing strategies for priority India-based government and defense accounts, mapping buying centers and orchestrating multi-touch engagement across events, executive briefings, targeted campaigns, and owned customer programs.
  • Demonstrate deep understanding of India’s defense procurement environment, acquisition cycles, and stakeholder landscape, tailoring engagement strategies accordingly.
  • Own strategy and execution for major India-based defense and aerospace events, prioritizing high-value account engagement and executive-level meetings over brand presence alone.
  • Plan and execute owned customer events, private briefings, and targeted engagements in partnership with sales and capture teams to accelerate opportunity progression.
  • Align marketing programs with local sales objectives, pipeline targets, and account plans to drive measurable revenue impact.
  • Own Salesforce campaign execution, including creating and managing Campaign IDs, ensuring proper campaign structure, tracking member status, and maintaining data integrity in partnership with sales.
  • Partner closely with sales to ensure correct opportunity association, contact mapping, and required CRM field completion to support accurate pipeline reporting and attribution.
  • Build and execute programs within Marketo, including campaign flows, audience segmentation, lead scoring alignment, and performance reporting.
  • Establish KPIs aligned to India government sales realities, including account penetration, stakeholder engagement depth, opportunity influence, and deal acceleration.
  • Bring structure, accountability, and operational rigor to field marketing execution within a fast-growing international market.

Required qualifications:

  • 8–12 years of experience in field marketing, demand generation, or integrated marketing roles
  • Proven expertise in account-based marketing (ABM) within complex enterprise or government accounts
  • Demonstrated ability to drive pipeline, influence opportunities, and support deal progression
  • Hands-on experience executing events, executive engagements, and targeted field programs
  • Strong experience working cross-functionally with Sales, BD, and capture teams
  • Hands-on Salesforce (SFDC) experience, including campaign creation, Campaign ID management, opportunity alignment, and pipeline reporting
  • Experience with marketing automation platforms (e.g., Marketo) for campaign execution, segmentation, and nurture programs
  • Ability to operate in a hands-on, high-growth environment with strong ownership and execution focus

Preferred qualifications:

  • Experience marketing into India government, Ministry of Defence, Armed Forces, DRDO, or defense/aerospace sectors
  • Strong understanding of India’s defense procurement processes and long-cycle acquisition environments
  • Experience aligning marketing programs to capture efforts for large, complex government deals
  • Familiarity with key Indian defense stakeholders and decision-making structures
  • Proficiency in Hindi or other regional Indian languages

#LE

allowance, equity, international benefits, visa assistance, and relocation assistance.

Salary compensation is influenced by a wide array of factors including but not limited to skill set, level of experience, licenses and certifications, and specific work location. All offers are contingent on a cleared background and possible reference check.

Shield AI is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

items.title: National Sales Manager - AVP - Enterprise Mid-Market - Noida
items.description:
Job Description — National Sales Manager (NSM)


Role Summary
As the National Sales Manager for the Enterprise team, you will be responsible for leading strategic sales initiatives, driving revenue growth across large corporate and enterprise accounts, and expanding Paytm’s footprint in the digital payments ecosystem. You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Product, Tech, Operations, and Risk to deliver tailored solutions and exceed commercial goals.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and manage the Enterprise sales function to achieve revenue targets across assigned markets.
Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with C-level stakeholders and decision-makers at large enterprise clients.
Drive new business acquisition while maximizing upsell and cross-sell opportunities within existing accounts.
Own the P&L and performance metrics for enterprise portfolios, ensuring consistent achievement of quarterly and annual sales objectives.
Collaborate with internal cross-functional teams (Product, Tech, Operations, Risk & Compliance) to tailor offerings and resolve escalations.
Lead contract negotiations, pricing discussions, and service level agreements with key accounts.
Analyze market trends, competitor activities, and performance data to inform strategic decision-making.
Mentor and guide regional sales managers or account leadership to cultivate a high-performance sales organization.

Required Qualifications & Skills

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, or related field.
10+ years of experience in enterprise sales, business development, or key account management—preferably within payments, fintech, or BFSI.
Proven track record of meeting or exceeding revenue targets in enterprise B2B environments.
Strong negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
Strategic mindset with ability to assess data and prioritize actionable insights.
Leadership experience managing high-performing sales teams.

Preferred Attributes

Deep understanding of payment gateway products, APIs, and integration ecosystems.
Experience working closely with product and technology teams to design enterprise solutions.
Ability to travel as required to engage with key accounts.

items.title: Associate Manager - Pricing
items.description:
About Inizio

Inizio is a global partner with over 10,500 experts operating in more than 50 countries. We support health and life science companies by turning science into strategy and executing across the product and patient journey. Our services include market research, strategy consulting, transformation consulting, and commercial benchmarking, all designed to be data-backed and patient-inspired.

Inizio Ignite, Research Partnership

Inizio Ignite is the global advisory partner for health and life sciences, encompassing connected expertise across Research Partnership, Putnam, Vynamic, and STEM. Guided by purpose, passion, and precision, we collaborate with clients to ignite impact, accelerate performance, and deliver lasting change for patients. By uniting strategy, insights, and innovation with a unique depth and breadth of expertise, we drive transformation and shape the future of health. Our Research Partnership healthcare market research team provides research and insights to inform and power healthcare decisions. Serving a variety of clients across the lifecycle, our expertise includes data, research & insights (proprietary syndicated and custom research) and brand tracking (ATU). Proudly part of Inizio – the market-leading commercialization partner for health and life sciences. From early-stage development to loss of exclusivity, we simplify complexity, unlock value, and make collaboration seamless at every pivotal moment – supporting confident, insight-driven decisions that move innovation forward. 

Role Overview

The Pricing Analyst plays a vital role in supporting the RP Pricing Team by assisting in the development of accurate and competitive costs for global healthcare market research projects. This role involves analyzing project specifications, collecting quotes from third partners, collaborating with internal teams, and ensuring pricing aligns with industry benchmarks and Research Partnership objectives. The Pricing Analyst will work on multi-market qualitative and quantitative studies, helping to optimize pricing strategies while maintaining profitability and efficiency. Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment are essential for success in this role.

Here’s what you’ll be doing:

Core, but not exclusive, responsibilities will include:

  • Owning the end-to-end pricing lifecycle for RP proposals, from RFQ intake and supplier outreach via the RFQ management system through to final cost proposal development. This includes defining and sourcing all cost components required for successful project delivery, including external vendor costs and internal resources and hours, to ensure accurate, comprehensive, and commercially sound proposals.
  • Vendor selection at RFQ stage and quote collection from third parties
  • Creation of internal cost sheets for client proposals, ensuring accuracy and financial viability
  • Completion of client cost sheets
  • Assistance with RFI client pricing exercises
  • Successful handover of final project cost sheets to The Project Management Team
  • Working closely with internal stakeholders, including sales, operations, and finance, to align pricing with business strategy

Here’s what you’ll need:

  • Strong commercial, analytical and organizational skills with attention to detail.
  • More than 5 years of experience in a role involving costs and negotiations or at market research agency
  • Significant experience with teamwork and collaboration
  • Strong project management skills (adhering to agreed timelines, troubleshooting and communication with internal and external stakeholders)
  • High level of accuracy and attention to detail
  • Confident liaising with a variety of internal and external stakeholders
  • Excellent Excel and numerical skills
  • Familiarity with inflation indices and pricing models is a plus.
  • Finance degree or relevant discipline degree.
  • Proactive, dedicated and enthusiastic ‘can do’ attitude and approach

Our Pledge

At Inizio, we value inclusivity, recognize the power of diversity, and inspire the next generation of change-makers. We are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a dynamic work environment that values diversity, equity, and inclusion. We welcome all applications regardless of race, color, religion, gender identity, age, national origin, marital status, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, or disability.

Artificial Intelligence

Inizio may use AI technologies to support certain aspects of our recruitment process, but all hiring decisions are made by our human recruiting team. We are committed to fair, inclusive, and human-led hiring practices. Don't meet every requirement? That's okay! We are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't perfectly align with every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway.

Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.
