items.title: FBS Agile Dev Team Member III - .NET Full Stack
items.description:
Participate in the coding, testing, and debugging of Optimizely CMS websites to ensure they meet specified requirements and quality standards. Apply best practices in web development, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, and testing. Optimize web applications for performance, responsiveness, and search engine optimization (SEO) to enhance our online presence. Develop high-quality, reusable code components or blocks using C#, and .NET. Design, develop, and maintain high-quality web applications using the .NET 6 framework, HTML5, CSS3, and knowledge in modern frontend stack like Node JS, NPM (Node Package Manager), Webpack. Collaborate with cross-functional teams such as design, analytics, product, and content. Experience in OKTA SAML setup. Engage in continuous learning to enhance technical skills and knowledge of Optimizely CMS, as well as other relevant technologies and development practices. Contribute to the documentation of project features and technical processes to support the maintenance and future development of projects. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship new features. Implement responsive user interfaces using TailwindCSS that work seamlessly across various devices and browsers. Utilize Azure DevOps for project management, version control, and continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD). Integrate with and consume APIs for data retrieval and system interoperability. Participate in code reviews to ensure code quality, performance, and adherence to coding standards. Work closely with 3rd party vendors on tasks or issues. Collaborate with our digital marketing team to understand project requirements, goals, and objectives specific to the individual business groups in Generac. Implement visually appealing front-end designs and user interfaces that align with Generac's brand identity and digital marketing strategies. Utilize Optimizely CMS for efficient content management and dynamic experiences on our websites. Integrate web applications with SAP, Salesforce and various marketing technologies, such as analytics tools, email marketing platforms, and personalization engines within Optimizely CMS 12 best practices Conduct thorough testing and debugging to ensure seamless functionality across different browsers, devices, and platforms. Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including designers, content creators, and marketing analysts, to deliver cohesive digital experiences. Stay up-to-date with emerging web development trends, tools, and technologies to recommend improvements and drive innovation in our digital marketing initiatives. Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to web applications and marketing integrations to ensure a seamless user experience.

SEO - Intermediate
Experimentation - Intermediate

Software / Tool Skills

Optimizely CMS - Intermediate (4-6 Years)
.NET 6 framework - Advanced (7+ Years)
HTML5 - Advanced (7+ Years)
CSS3 - Advanced (7+ Years)
Node JS, NPM (Node Package Manager), Webpack - Advanced (7+ Years)

Competitive compensation and benefits package:

  1. Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
  2. Comprehensive benefits package
  3. Career development and training opportunities
  4. Flexible work arrangements (remote and/or office-based)
  5. Dynamic and inclusive work culture within a globally renowned group
  6. Private Health Insurance
  7. Pension Plan
  8. Paid Time Off
  9. Training & Development

Note: Benefits differ based on employee level.

About Capgemini

Capgemini is a global leader in partnering with companies to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology. The Group is guided everyday by its purpose of unleashing human energy through technology for an inclusive and sustainable future. It is a responsible and diverse organization of over 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong 55-year heritage and deep industry expertise, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to address the entire breadth of their business needs, from strategy and design to operations, fueled by the fast evolving and innovative world of cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering and platforms. The Group €22.5 billion in revenues in 2023.

https://www.capgemini.com/us-en/about-us/who-we-are/

items.title: Group FP&A Senior Analyst
items.description:
Keywords Studios, established in Dublin in 1998, now has 70+ studios across Europe, North and South America and Asia with 13,000 employee strength located across 5 continents and 26 countries. The company provides a complete outsourced game art, engineering, testing, audio and localization service for all Console, PC, Handheld and Mobile content, to many of the biggest names in games and interactive entertainment, working on thousands of titles including many of the best-selling titles of the past few years.

Keywords Studios is comprised of many individual brands, all with something unique to offer our clients. The studios are integrated into the Group by Service Line and use the operating systems and tools deployed by those services lines to ensure people and projects can operate across studios and across geographies.

For more info, please refer to www.keywordsstudios.com

Job Overview:

Working closely with the Head of Group FP&A, this role will take part in the preparation of financial reporting to the KWS Executive and Board, ensuring delivery to the highest standards of accuracy and insightfulness. Under the direction of the Head of Group FP&A, the role will also assist in the preparation and consolidation of annual budgeting, long range planning and near term forecasting processes, ensuring that stretching but achievable targets are developed and embedded across the organization. The role will assist in the preparation of KPIs/KLIs, financial models and strategic analysis to support decision making by KWS Executives and PE investors. The role will work alongside other Finance team members to help ensure that Group and Divisional reporting and planning is standardized and automated and be a strong advocate for our Finance Excellence vision to be trusted business partners that champion growth, create value and manage risk.

Key Responsibilities:

The Group FP&A Senior Analyst will deliver the following key activities:

Group FP&A Process, Systems & Data

  • Input to the development KWS FP&A planning and reporting processes, standardizing financial processes, models and KPIs and driving consistent adoption across the Finance organization.
  • Assist the preparation of monthly Exec and Board financial reporting and KPIs, consolidating submissions from Finance teams and providing clear, insightful management reporting and dashboards, in-depth financial analysis and actionable insights to support decision-making.
  • Support activities to develop and communicate the timelines and targets associated with the annual budget, forecasts and long range planning processes, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and market conditions.
  • Support the preparation of the Risks & Opportunities process and consolidating and challenging inputs from divisions.
  • Input to the preparation of budget, forecasts and long range plans, consolidating and assessing the inputs received from the Finance teams and preparing related Executive and Board presentations.
  • Contribute to activities that will ensure financial systems and tools are utilized to streamline reporting and analysis, helping to ensure their integration across FP&A, Divisions and Controlling teams to ensure consistency and accuracy of all Group FP&A team outputs.
  • Provide the Group FP&A team with inputs to the governance around the Finance data model, reporting standards and reporting tools used across the Finance function, ensuring that requests for analysis is capable of being delivered without significant manual effort.
  • Develop financial models to support long-term planning including use of driver-based forecasting approaches.
  • Recommend ways to improve forecasting accuracy through better data, automation, and analytics.
  • Contribute to activities to drive continuous improvement and scalability of FP&A operations.

Partnering with Executives and PE Investors

  • Support the Head of Group FP&A by developing financial models and analysis that enables insight and high quality decision support to Executives and PE Investors to drive growth and business performance. Support the preparation of management financial reporting, analysis and insightful commentary.
  • Work with the Head of Group FP&A to respond to requests from investors and and other external stakeholders for analysis and reporting on business strategy and performance.
  • Support the Head of Group FP&A in translating business strategy into financial plans and measurable targets.
  • Provide appropriate financial support to the CFO and wider teams as required.

Strategic Analysis and Action Planning

  • As directed, build robust financial models and develop strategic analysis to identify opportunities for growth and to mitigate risk.
  • Input to research and the development of proposals for strategic initiatives aimed at delivering growth and responding to emerging threats and opportunities.
  • Contribute to the delivery of analysis in areas including; revenue, margin and cost drivers; customer and product profitability, unit economics and ROI.
  • Prepare scenario modeling and sensitivity analysis.

Risk Management & Scenario Planning

  • Take part in activities to Identify financial risks and stress-test plans.
  • Support the modeling of downside, upside, and contingency scenarios. Support liquidity planning and robust cash flow forecasting.

Finance Team Management and Collaboration

  • Work as key member of the Group FP&A team and the wider Finance team, helping to shape, advocate for and deliver our Finance Excellence vision to be trusted business partners that champion growth, create value and manage risk.

Job Requirements

This is an intermediate level role in the organization that requires a capable Senior Finance Analyst with relevant experience in assisting the delivery of Global FP&A planning and reporting processes in organizations that are fast moving and need attention to decisions on contract pricing and margins. The role is expected to have strong financial modeling and analysis skills and ability to present results and commentary that is highly visual and insightful. To be successful in the role it is expected that you will have:

  • Finance MBA or Part Qualified Bachelor's Degree in Accounting.
  • 3 - 4 years experience working as a Senior Analyst / Analyst within the Group FP&A function of a complex, multinational organization.
  • A good knowledge of building out and utilising PowerBI dashboards and other dashboarding tools as we develop more data-led reporting and forecasting.
  • High degree of data literacy and familiarity with BI tools and analysing complex data sets to identify trends and opportunities.
  • Experience of supporting financial reporting, planning, budgeting, and forecasting processes with high attention to detail, insightfulness and accuracy.
  • Highly proficient in financial modelling skills including the use of financial planning tools, BI and Excel.
  • Able to communicate effectively with Finance Leaders.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  • Natural collaborative and inclusive approach to working with Finance team members and colleagues.

Role Information: IN

Studio: Location: Keywords India

Area of Work: Finance

Service: Support

Employment Type: Permanent

Working Pattern: In-Office

items.title: Director of Digital Communications
items.description:
Is this you? 

 You're an experienced MarComms professional with a track record in the SaaS - Tech sector. You take initiative without being asked. You move fast, think clearly, and know how to get things done across channels, teams, and competing priorities. You embrace AI as a force multiplier — not a shortcut — and you're always looking for smarter ways to work.

  • Self-starter with high drive and energy
  • Collaborative team player who also thrives independently
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail
  • Tech-savvy and quick to adopt new tools
  • Focused on outcomes, not just outputs

 

This is Preservica

A fast growing SaaS solution provider, Preservica is changing the way organisations safeguard and protect long-term digital information. A recognised leader in active digital preservation, our award-winning SaaS software is trusted by a rapidly growing user base of over 1,000 organisations worldwide. 

Focusing on information governance and the archival market, Preservica's key differentiation is that we future-proof information for the long-term, ensuring it remains readable and operationally usable for decades to come. Our customers need to retain information for 10+ years — or indefinitely — across a wide variety of file formats including documents, images, video, email, GIS, social media and more.

 

The Opportunity

In this role, you will own Preservica's content and communications engine - driving visibility, engagement, and demand generation across our key vertical markets. You will lead a modern, AI-powered content and communications strategy that puts Preservica in front of buyers wherever they are researching — from AI search tools like Gemini, Copilot and ChatGPT, to LinkedIn feeds, email inboxes, and trade publications. You won't just be aware of how AI is reshaping buyer behaviour — you'll be ahead of it, building programmes that capitalize on it.

This is a hands-on, execution-focused role for someone who already utilizes AI tools daily to optimize and scale output without sacrificing quality or brand voice – understanding that great content and smart distribution are at the heart of modern B2B demand generation.

 

What You'll Own

This role owns Preservica's content and marketing communications output, with a strong bias toward digital, AI-optimized, and ABM-aligned execution. You will be responsible for building and running a coherent, high-velocity content and communications program that supports pipeline growth across our vertical markets. You will own a portion of the marketing budget and oversee a small team, working closely with supporting agencies to plan, produce, and distribute content at scale.

GEO & AI Search Strategy

  • Build and execute a content strategy optimised for AI-powered search tools (Google Gemini, Copilot, ChatGPT), ensuring Preservica surfaces where buyers research.
  • Align with traditional SEO and use AI tools to scale output without sacrificing quality.

ABM & LinkedIn

  • Develop content and campaign assets supporting ABM programs across priority verticals
  • Own Preservica's LinkedIn strategy and broader social media presence, driving persona-level engagement that builds pipeline

Lead Generation & Nurture

  • Work closely with Marketing Demand Managers to create and manage lead gen content (tip sheets, infographics, email sequences, landing pages) for key personas across verticals
  • Own the editorial calendar and partner with Demand Generation to feed nurture workflows and support conversion goals.

Core Marcomms

  • Produce press materials, blogs, whitepapers, case studies, and product announcements
  • Develop and evolve Preservica's messaging frameworks, translating complex topics in digital preservation and information governance into compelling narratives

 PR & Media Relations

  • Appoint & manage PR agency relationships to drive thought leadership in vertical trade publications and build direct relationships with key journalists and analysts

Team & Agency Management

  • Line manage a small team, brief and hold agencies accountable, and act as a communications advisor to senior leadership

 

What You Bring 

  • 7+ years in B2B content or marketing communications, preferably in SaaS or tech
  • Working knowledge of GEO and how AI search is reshaping B2B buyer behavior + prove it
  • Exceptional writer who adapts voice and format across audiences and channels
  • Strong LinkedIn organic and paid expertise, including ABM-aligned targeting
  • Proven ability to manage and hold external PR agencies accountable to results
  • Solid grasp of digital communications: social, email, and SEO
  • Experience managing direct reports with a collaborative, empowering style
  • Hands-on with marketing automation (HubSpot or Pardot preferred) for email, nurture, and reporting
  • Fluent with AI writing tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Jasper, etc.) to scale content production
  • Data-driven, with comfort in Google Analytics, LinkedIn Insights, HubSpot, or Salesforce
  • Highly organized — able to juggle multiple projects in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment
  • BA or equivalent

 

Nice to Have

  • Familiarity with government, archives, higher ed, financial services, or records management.
  • ABM platform experience or structured ABM campaign execution.
  • Experience supporting executive thought leadership and spokesperson programs.
  • Background in digital preservation, information governance, or enterprise content management.

 

What We Offer

  • Attractive base salary & company bonus
  • Comprehensive benefits: medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance
  • Flexible, open vacation policy
  • Full remote / work from home

 

Preservica are an equal opportunities employer.

items.title: Technology Risk & Controls Manager, TechOps
items.description:
WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. 

We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. 
 
We have been building the world's most valuable brands for 50 years and have global reach across 100+ markets, with deep local expertise.
 
Our people are the key to our success. We're committed to fostering a culture of creativity, belonging and continuous learning, attracting and developing the brightest talent, and providing exciting career opportunities that help our people grow. 
 
For more information, visit WPP.com.
 

Why we're hiring:

The Cyber Risk and Compliance team ensures that WPP Creative Technology Operations adhere to internal policies, standards and control requirements to meet regulatory, client and external audit obligations.

This role is primarily focused on providing second‑line assurance over Technology Operations, including infrastructure, platforms, identity, service management and operational processes, ensuring controls are designed appropriately and operating effectively.

The role is pivotal in embedding robust Data & Technology controls across Tech Ops, supporting audit readiness (including SOX 404), and driving more consistent, standardised and repeatable operational control practices. Working closely with Financial Risk & Control and Technology Operations leadership, the role ensures Tech Ops teams are equipped to demonstrate control performance clearly and consistently.

 

What you'll be doing:

  • Provide second‑line assurance over Technology Operations control environments, including infrastructure, cloud platforms, identity and access management, service management and operational processes
  • Act as a key risk and controls partner to Technology Operations leadership, providing independent oversight, challenge and assurance
  • Perform control design and operating effectiveness reviews across Tech Ops processes, including change, access, incident, problem, backup and resilience controls
  • Lead and support internal, external and client audits primarily impacting Technology Operations, acting as the central point of coordination and assurance
  • Monitor, challenge and support remediation of audit deficiencies relating to Tech Ops control failures, ensuring sustainable fixes rather than point solution
  • Be a key liaison with the markets to assist in timely completion of audits (internal, external or client), providing support and guidance as required
  • Communicate effectively with Control Owners, Operators, Business platform Directors and other senior stakeholders.
  • Ensure all stakeholders are informed about progress, risks, and issues.
  • Develop Standard Operating Procedures and Risk and Controls Matrix for application control environment
  • Play a key role in monitoring audit deficiencies - from challenging the validity of deficiencies in audit close meetings, to supporting markets in developing remediation plans
  • Review remediation documentation to ensure plan has been implemented correctly
  • Identify appropriate mitigating controls for audit deficiencies, considering both controls on the IT and financial framework which adequately addresses the risk identified
  • Perform detailed walkthroughs of automated controls, to review system configuration and ensure risk associated to the control has been addressed
  • Provide up to date reporting of audit readiness to the Director
  •  Line management of Technology Risk & Controls Analysts, including objective setting, performance management and ongoing development
  • Provide day‑to‑day direction, coaching and technical oversight to analyst team members across audits, control reviews and remediation activity
  • Allocate work across the analyst team to ensure efficient coverage of audit, SOX and client assurance requirements
  • Review analyst deliverables to ensure quality, consistency and alignment with WPP control standards and assurance methodologies
  •  Act as an escalation point for complex control, audit or stakeholder issues raised by analysts
  •  Support capability development within the analyst team, including coaching on SOX, ITGCs, application controls and audit best practice
  • Contribute to building a high‑performing, collaborative team culture focused on accountability, learning and continuous improvement

What you'll need:

  • Strong understanding of Technology Operations control environments, including ITGCs, platform operations, IAM, infrastructure, cloud and service management controls
  • Experience providing assurance over run‑the‑business technology functions, rather than solely project or change initiatives
  • Ability to challenge Technology Operations teams constructively on control design, evidence quality and sustainability
  • Hands‑on experience reviewing operational processes such as change management, access management, incident/problem management and availability/resilience
  • Understands the importance of strong IT controls and maintaining a positive user experience
  • Prior experience in internal and external IT audits
  • Robust understanding in SOX controls framework. Experience working with external auditors on SOX compliance within large-scale transformation programmes.
  • Ability to work independently and proactively work on solutions to self-identified problems
  • Attention to detail - Applying quality standards to all tasks undertaken and ensuring that nothing is overlooked.
  • Ability to collaborate with business and technology stakeholders to explain the technology risk implications, and communicate in a language that is appropriate to the audience
  • Ability to adjust priorities and manage time appropriately and wisely in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships across departments and global markets
  • Highly organised with ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and to analyse data effectively, efficiently and accurately.
  • Prior exposure to data migration assurance and reconciliation processes.
  • Relevant experience and or professional certifications (e.g., CISA, CIA, CRISC) are advantageous.
  • Fluency in Spanish is essential for this role
  • Demonstrable experience managing, coaching or mentoring analysts or junior team members in a risk, audit or compliance environment
  • Ability to translate complex audit and control requirements into clear, actionable guidance for analyst teams
  • Strong review and quality assurance skills, with the ability to challenge work constructively and raise standards
  • Confidence in prioritising and balancing team workloads across multiple concurrent audits or initiatives
  • Effective leadership and stakeholder management skills, including handling escalation, feedback and performance conversations
  • Structured and methodical approach to developing team capability and embedding consistent ways of working Ability to lead by example in a fast‑paced, high‑expectation, global environment

 

Who you are:

You're open: We are inclusive and collaborative; we encourage the free exchange of ideas; we respect and celebrate diverse views. We are open-minded: to new ideas, new partnerships, new ways of working.

You're optimistic: We believe in the power of creativity, technology and talent to create brighter futures or our people, our clients and our communities. We approach all that we do with conviction: to try the new and to seek the unexpected.

You're extraordinary: we are stronger together: through collaboration we achieve the amazing. We are creative leaders and pioneers of our industry; we provide extraordinary every day.

 

What we'll give you:

Passionate, inspired people – We aim to create a culture in which people can do extraordinary work.

Scale and opportunity – We offer the opportunity to create, influence and complete projects at a scale that is unparalleled in the industry.

Challenging and stimulating work – Unique work and the opportunity to join a group of creative problem solvers. Are you up for the challenge?

#LI-Hybrid 

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

WPP is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

Please read our Privacy Notice (https://www.wpp.com/en/careers/wpp-privacy-policy-for-recruitment) for more information on how we process the information you provide.

items.title: Data Analyst
items.description:
Position: Data Analyst

Location: Mumbai, India

About LRN:

LRN is the world’s leading dedicated ethics and compliance SaaS company, helping more than 30 million people every year navigate complex regional and global regulatory environments and build ethical, responsible cultures. With over 3,000 clients across the US, EMEA, APAC, and Latin America—including some of the world’s most respected and successful brands—we’re proud to be the long-term partner trusted to reduce organizational risk and drive principled performance. 

Named one of Inc Magazine’s 5000 Fastest-Growing Companies, LRN is redefining how organizations turn values into action. Our state-of-the-art platform combines intuitive design, mobile accessibility, robust analytics, and industry benchmarking—enabling organizations to create, manage, deliver, and audit ethics and compliance programs with confidence. Backed by a unique blend of technology, education, and expert advisement, LRN helps companies turn their values into real-world behaviors and leadership practices that deliver lasting competitive advantage. 

About the role:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and commercially aware Data Analyst to support LRN’s Commercial Strategy function. This role focuses on analysing data and translating it into clear, structured insights that inform decision-making across pricing, go-to-market strategy, and revenue performance. 

You will play a key role in supporting core commercial processes, including price book management, win/loss analysis, and performance tracking, ensuring data is accurate, well-structured, and actionable. 

Working in a fast-paced and evolving environment, you will partner closely with stakeholders across Sales, Product, and Finance, helping to respond to shifting business priorities while maintaining a strong analytical foundation. 

What you'll do:

Data Analysis & Insight Development 

  • Structure, manipulate, and validate data from multiple sources to ensure consistency, accuracy, and usability 
  • Analyse revenue, pricing, pipeline, and customer data to identify trends, patterns, and key performance drivers 
  • Conduct win/loss analysis to understand drivers of deal outcomes, including pricing, product fit, competition, and sales approach 
  • Own price book analysis and support ongoing updates, ensuring pricing structures are accurate, competitive, and well-governed 
  • Investigate performance across retention, upsell, churn, and new business acquisition to identify key drivers of growth 
  • Translate complex datasets into clear, concise summaries and insights to support strategic discussions and decision-making 
  • Support analysis for key commercial initiatives, including product launches and market assessments 
  • Build a strong understanding of core commercial metrics and how they link to overall business performance 

Stakeholder Support 

  • Work with Commercial Strategy, Sales, Finance and Product teams to deliver data and analysis for ongoing projects  
  • Respond to data requests with structured outputs and clear interpretation  
  • Contribute to presentations and materials for internal stakeholders  

Data & Reporting Infrastructure 

  • Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and reporting tools (e.g., Salesforce, Domo, BI platforms)  
  • Ensure consistency and accuracy of key commercial data  
  • Help automate and streamline reporting processes 

What we're looking for: 

  • 4+ years in a data analyst, business analyst, or similar role 
  • Bachelor’s degree (BA/BS) in a relevant field (e.g., Economics, Mathematics, Business, Data Analytics, or similar) required 
  • Certifications in relevant tools or methodologies (e.g., Alteryx, Domo, Salesforce, Excel, SQL) are preferred and demonstrate technical competency 
  • Experience in SaaS, technology, or data-driven environments preferred 
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail  
  • Experience working with commercial or sales data, ideally in a SaaS environment  
  • Advanced Excel skills, with experience in tools such as Domo, Alteryx, and Salesforce  
  • Ability to structure, manipulate, and analyse data from multiple sources to generate clear, actionable insights  
  • Familiarity with AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Copilot) to support analysis, research, and workflow efficiency  
  • Interest in applying emerging technologies to enhance analysis while maintaining accuracy and critical thinking 
  • Excellent medical benefits, including family plan
  • Paid Time Off (PTO) plus India public holidays 
  • Competitive salary
  • Combined Onsite and Remote Work

LRN is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

items.title: Manager - Social Analyst
items.description:
About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth. 

WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

About WPP Media 

WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com. 

WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com 

At WPP Media, we believe in the power of our culture and our people. It’s what elevates us to deliver exceptional experiences for both our clients and each other. In this role it will be critical to embrace WPP & WPP Media’s shared core values: 

  • Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity. 
  • Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust. 
  • Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise. 

 

Overview of Job:    

As a Social Media Analyst in Gurgaon, your role revolves around conducting comprehensive social listening and sentiment analysis across various social media platforms. This involves identifying key trends, consumer sentiments, and brand mentions for the brand.   

Reporting Structure:   

 This position reports to the Group Head.   

Top 3 Aspects of the Job:   

  • Dive into in-depth social listening and sentiment analysis for a range of brands.
  • Utilize advanced analytics tools to interpret and present social media data in actionable reports.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop data-driven social media campaigns and influencer partnerships.

In three months:
The candidate will have established a deep understanding of our current social media analytics tools and processes. They will actively contribute to ongoing projects, demonstrating proficiency in data analysis and providing valuable insights.   

In six months:
The candidate is expected to lead initiatives aimed at optimizing social media strategies based on data-driven insights. This includes implementing improvements to measurement frameworks and collaborating with cross-functional teams to enhance overall social media performance.   

In twelve months:
The individual in this role will have played a pivotal role in elevating the social analytics function. They will have successfully implemented advanced analytics techniques, contributed to strategic decision-making, and fostered a culture of continuous improvement within the team.  

Responsibilities:   

  • Conduct in-depth social listening and sentiment analysis across various social media platforms.
  • Utilize advanced analytics tools to interpret and present social media data in clear, actionable reports.
  • Create and distribute periodic social and media reports for leadership, including daily, weekly, and quarterly reports, or custom reports as needed.
  • Monitor and analyze social media conversations to identify emerging opportunities, potential risks, and competitor movements.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to strategize and develop data-driven social media campaigns.
  • Manage the influencer marketing process, from vetting potential influencers to evaluating the success of campaigns.
  • Monitor influencer campaigns and measure their impact on brand awareness, engagement, and conversion rates.
  • Stay up-to-date with industry best practices, social media trends, and emerging technologies to enhance the effectiveness of social listening and influencer marketing efforts.

Requirements:   

  • Minimum of 5 - 6 years of experience in social media analytics and social listening.   
  • Proficiency in using social media analytics tools and platforms, such as Sprout Social, Hootsuite, Brandwatch, Talkwalker, or similar.   
  • Proven track record of successfully identifying social insights and trends, translating data into actionable business recommendations.   
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to derive valuable insights from complex datasets.   
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to communicate data-driven findings to stakeholders at all levels.   
  • Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.   

Life at WPP Media & Benefits 

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising. We have a variety of employee resource groups and host frequent in-office events showcasing team wins, sharing thought leadership, and celebrating holidays and milestone events. Our benefits include competitive medical, group retirement plans, vision, and dental insurance, significant paid time off, preferential partner discounts, and employee mental health awareness days.   

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

items.title: Growth Management - Manager - Recharges & Bill Payments
items.description:
About Paytm Group: 

Paytm is India's leading mobile payments and financial services distribution company. Pioneer of the mobile QR payments revolution in India, Paytm builds technologies that help small businesses with payments and commerce. Paytm’s mission is to serve half a billion Indians and bring them to the mainstream economy with the help of technology.

Job Title: Manager/Senior Manager – Growth & Business Analytics

Business Unit: Recharges & Utilities (RU)

About the Team:

Recharges and Utilities is a core business pillar for Paytm, encompassing 20+ essential categories including Mobile Recharge, Electricity, DTH, and Loans As a Manager in this team, you will be the 'Growth Engine'—using deep data insights to identify opportunities and executing high-impact campaigns to acquire and retain millions of users.

The Role: Analytical Execution & Category Growth

We are looking for a high-ownership individual who is equally comfortable writing SQL queries in Hive as they are designing a marketing campaign. You will own the data lifecycle of your assigned categories and use that data to drive P&L growth and user retention.

Core Roles & Responsibilities

1. Data Operations & Analytics (The 'How')
Data Extraction: Perform daily data extraction from HIVE/BigQuery and prepare user-readable transaction reports.
Query Management: Write and optimize SQL queries on a day-to-day basis to track business health and perform ad-hoc deep dives.
Automation: Use Advanced Excel and internal tools to automate recurring reports, streamlining MIS processes for the leadership team.
Data Modeling: Translate business hurdles (e.g., 'Why is electricity bill churn increasing?') into data models to find actionable solutions.

2. Growth & Category Management (The 'What')

P&L Support: Assist in delivering revenue targets by optimizing costs and identifying bottom-line enhancing strategies.
User Lifecycle: Identify specific user cohorts (e.g., 'lapsed rent payers') and execute custom growth campaigns to drive activation and reactivation.
Funnel Optimization: Monitor the user journey from landing to transaction; identify drop-off points and suggest/implement funnel improvements.
Retention & GMV: Track and take ownership of key parameters including Daily Active Users (DAU), GMV, and Month-on-Month (MoM) retention.

3. Marketing & Campaign Execution

ROI Analysis: Measure the performance of every campaign, ensuring that marketing spend is driving efficient growth.
Competitive Intelligence: Keep a pulse on market trends and competitor offerings to ensure Paytm’s utility categories remain market leaders.

Superpowers & Skills Required

•Experience: 2-4 years of experience in a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or •Growth role (preferably within E-commerce, Retail, or Fintech).
Technical Proficiency: 
•Advanced Excel (VBA/Macros is a plus) and strong SQL skills.
•Experience with Hive, BigQuery (BQ), or Python for data manipulation.
•Analytical Mindset: Background in Engineering, Math, Statistics, or Economics. •You should be able to see the 'story' behind the numbers.
•Execution Grit: Ability to follow through on projects, coordinating with cross-functional teams (Product, Tech, Marketing) to ensure tasks reach the finish line.
•Communication: Clear and effective communication skills to share updates with stakeholders and senior management.

Why join us

• A collaborative output driven program that brings cohesiveness across businesses through technology·
• Improve the average revenue per use by increasing the cross-sell opportunities·
• A solid 360 feedback from your peer teams on your support of their goals·
• Respect, that is earned, not demanded from your peers and manager.

Compensation:

If you are the right fit, we believe in creating wealth for you With enviable 500 mn+ registered users, 17 mn+ merchants and depth of data in our ecosystem, we are in a unique position to democratize credit for deserving consumers & merchants – and we are committed to it. India’s largest digital lending story is brewing here. It’s your opportunity to be a part of the story!
About Paytm Group: 

Paytm is India's leading mobile payments and financial services distribution company. Pioneer of the mobile QR payments revolution in India, Paytm builds technologies that help small businesses with payments and commerce. Paytm’s mission is to serve half a billion Indians and bring them to the mainstream economy with the help of technology.

Job Title: Manager/Senior Manager – Growth & Business Analytics

Business Unit: Recharges & Utilities (RU)

About the Team:

Recharges and Utilities is a core business pillar for Paytm, encompassing 20+ essential categories including Mobile Recharge, Electricity, DTH, and Loans As a Manager in this team, you will be the 'Growth Engine'—using deep data insights to identify opportunities and executing high-impact campaigns to acquire and retain millions of users.

The Role: Analytical Execution & Category Growth

We are looking for a high-ownership individual who is equally comfortable writing SQL queries in Hive as they are designing a marketing campaign. You will own the data lifecycle of your assigned categories and use that data to drive P&L growth and user retention.

Core Roles & Responsibilities

1. Data Operations & Analytics (The 'How')
Data Extraction: Perform daily data extraction from HIVE/BigQuery and prepare user-readable transaction reports.
Query Management: Write and optimize SQL queries on a day-to-day basis to track business health and perform ad-hoc deep dives.
Automation: Use Advanced Excel and internal tools to automate recurring reports, streamlining MIS processes for the leadership team.
Data Modeling: Translate business hurdles (e.g., 'Why is electricity bill churn increasing?') into data models to find actionable solutions.

3. Marketing & Campaign Execution

items.title: Operations Manager
items.description:
ABOUT TIDE

At Tide we help SMEs save time (and money) in the running of their businesses by not only offering business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of highly usable and connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.

Tide is transforming the small business banking market with over 1.8 million members globally across the UK, India, Germany and France.

Using advanced technology, all solutions are designed with SMEs in mind. With quick onboarding, low fees and innovative features, we thrive on making data-driven decisions to serve our mission: to help SMEs save both time (and money) so they can get back to doing what they love.

Tide facts:

  • Tide is available for UK, Indian, German and French SMEs
  • Over 1.8 million members: 800,000 UK and 1,000,000 in India and growing rapidly
  • Over $300 million raised in funding
  • Over 2,500 Tideans globally - we’re diversity champions!
  • We have offices in Central London, with a member support and technology centre in Sofia, Bulgaria, technology centres in Serbia, Romania, Lithuania and Hyderabad and offices in Gurugram and New Delhi, and in Berlin, Paris and Luxembourg.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The Admin Services team builds and manages tools for small and medium enterprises (SMEs), simplifying accounting, tax compliance, invoicing, bookkeeping, transaction enrichment, payroll, and expense management. By automating administrative work, we help SMEs save time, reduce complexity, and operate with confidence - so they can focus on growth. 

We are looking for an experienced and motivated Operations Manager to lead this mission.

In this role, you’ll help build, scale, and maintain products, collaborating with product owners, data analysts, support teams, developers, and external partners to drive operational excellence and establish scalable practices - all with a focus on delivering maximum value to our members! 

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

  • Establish and optimize L3 support escalation workflows, acting as the primary PoC for product-related issues and driving rapid resolution across Engineering, Product, and Support teams.
  • Own end-to-end operational processes, ensuring workflows across stakeholders are stable, efficient, well-documented, and scalable.
  • Partner with Product owners on Go-To-Market (GTM) initiatives to enable seamless launches of new features with minimal risk and member disruption.
  • Identify operational risks and inefficiencies, collaborating with Engineering to implement safeguards and contingency strategies.
  • Provide operational support during incidents, map critical dependencies, contribute to root cause analysis, and implement monitoring to reduce recurrence.
  • Define and track KPIs, partnering with Product and Data teams to build dashboards, reports, and alerts for effective product health monitoring.
  • Manage external vendors by overseeing SLAs, tracking performance metrics, and resolving third-party issues to ensure optimal outcomes.
  • Mentor and develop Operations Analysts and Senior Operations Analysts, setting objectives and fostering continuous growth and performance.
  • Cultivate a high-performance, data-driven culture, influencing cross-functional stakeholders and driving measurable business impact.
  • Support talent acquisition by contributing to hiring and interview processes to scale and strengthen the operations team.

YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF

  • You have 5+ years of experience in operations, technology, or finance, with a proven track record of thriving in a challenging and a fast-paced environment.
  • You are experienced in managing and resolving escalations, ensuring timely outcomes and effective cross-functional coordination
  • You view data as the core of decision-making. You are proficient in Excel/Google Sheets, SQL, and Looker for performance monitoring and automation.
  • You can move from deep-dive technical troubleshooting with developers to high-level strategic reporting for stakeholders.
  • You enjoy designing processes from scratch, spotting potential bottlenecks before they happen, and embedding "lessons learned" into the team's DNA.
  • You understand the concept of APIs and webhooks and how they can be utilized to automate and streamline complex business processes. You are comfortable with ambiguity, thrive in complex technical environments, and can influence others to drive the right business outcomes.

WHAT YOU WILL GET IN RETURN: 

Our location-specific employee benefits are designed to cater to the unique needs of Tideans:

 

  • Competitive Compensation -  competitive salary and share options
  • Time Off – Generous annual leave on top of bank holidays.
  • Parental Leave – Paid maternity, paternity, and adoption leave to support your family journey.
  • Sabbatical – Extended unpaid and paid leave options after completing milestone years with Tide.
  • Health Insurance – Private family insurance with additional OPD coverage and top-up options.
  • Life & Accident Cover – Comprehensive accidental and life insurance protection.
  • Mental Wellbeing – Access to therapy sessions, courses, meditations, and workshops.
  • Volunteering & Development Days – Paid days annually for volunteering or personal growth.
  • Learning & Development – Annual budget for books, courses, coaching, and more.
  • WOO (Work Outside the Office) – Work from abroad for up to 90 days annually.
  • Home Office Setup – Contribution towards setting up your home office
  • Laptop Ownership – Keep your old laptop and get a new one when it’s time for a replacement.
  • Snacks & Meals – Office perks with snacks, coffee, tea, and lunch (location dependent).

TIDEAN WAYS OF WORKING:

At Tide, we champion a flexible workplace model that supports both in-person and remote work to cater to the specific needs of our different teams. 

While remote work is supported, we believe in the power of face-to-face interactions to foster team spirit and collaboration. Our offices are designed as hubs for innovation and team-building, where we encourage regular in-person gatherings to foster a strong sense of community.


#LI-SW1

TIDE IS A PLACE FOR EVERYONE

At Tide, we believe that we can only succeed if we let our differences enrich our culture. Our Tideans come from a variety of backgrounds and experience levels. We consider everyone irrespective of their ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity or differently-abled status. We celebrate diversity in our workforce as a cornerstone of our success. Our commitment to a broad spectrum of ideas and backgrounds is what enables us to build products that resonate with our members’ diverse needs and lives. 

We are One Team and foster a transparent and inclusive environment, where everyone’s voice is heard.

 

At Tide, we thrive on diversity, embracing various backgrounds and experiences. We welcome all individuals regardless of ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Our inclusive culture is key to our success, helping us build products that meet our members' diverse needs. We are One Team, committed to transparency and ensuring everyone’s voice is heard.

 
Disclaimer
 
It has come to our attention that individuals or agencies are falsely claiming to represent Tide and are reaching out to candidates regarding job opportunities. Please be aware that:
  • Tide does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process.
  • All official Tide job opportunities are listed exclusively on our Careers Page and applications should be submitted through this channel.
  • Communication from Tide will only come from an official @tide.co email address.
  • Tide does not work with agencies or recruiters without prior formal engagement, and we do not authorize third parties to make job offers on our behalf.

If you are contacted by anyone misrepresenting Tide or requesting payment, please treat it as fraudulent and report it to us immediately at talent@tide.co
Your safety and trust are important to us, and we are committed to ensuring a fair and transparent recruitment process.

 

Tide leverages AI to enhance our hiring experience. You can read more about how we use AI in our recruitment process in our AI Policy.

Your personal data will be processed by Tide for recruitment purposes and in accordance with Tide's Recruitment Privacy Notice.

items.title: Senior Data Delivery & Dashboard Executive
items.description:
About Inizio Ignite:

Inizio Ignite is the global advisory partner for health and life sciences, encompassing connected expertise across Research Partnership, Putnam, Vynamic, and STEM. Guided by purpose, passion, and precision, we collaborate with clients to ignite impact, accelerate performance, and deliver lasting change for patients. By uniting strategy, insights, and innovation with a unique depth and breadth of expertise, we drive transformation and shape the future of health.

Company Descriptor

Inizio Ignite is the global advisory partner for health and life sciences, encompassing connected expertise across Research Partnership, Putnam, Vynamic, and STEM. Guided by purpose, passion, and precision, we collaborate with clients to ignite impact, accelerate performance, and deliver lasting change for patients. By uniting strategy, insights, and innovation with a unique depth and breadth of expertise, we drive transformation and shape the future of health. Our Research Partnership healthcare market research team provides research and insights to inform and power healthcare decisions. Serving a variety of clients across the lifecycle, our expertise includes data, research & insights (proprietary syndicated and custom research) and brand tracking (ATU). Proudly part of Inizio – the market-leading commercialization partner for health and life sciences. From early-stage development to loss of exclusivity, we simplify complexity, unlock value, and make collaboration seamless at every pivotal moment – supporting confident, insight-driven decisions that move innovation forward. 

 

Role Overview

The Data Delivery & Dashboards Executive role will report into the Dashboard Manager. The Data Delivery & Dashboards Executive will be a key member of the newly formed India-based Data Management & Delivery Hub, supporting quantitative healthcare market research projects across global markets. As a Dashboard Developer Executive, you build and maintain high impact dashboards and data visualizations that transform raw market research data into actionable insights. You’ll collaborate with researchers, analysts, and engineers to ensure data flows seamlessly from collections to visual storytelling. You will also support the team in delivering insightful, user-friendly dashboards through automated tools and off-the-shelf solutions. This is a fantastic opportunity to develop your skills in data management, quality assurance, and data visualisation, while working alongside an experienced team in a dynamic, global environment. Skill Set required

What you'll be doing

Brand Equity tracking support with our in-house platform:

  • Maintain the pipeline for the project data process flow (this includes receival of data into in-hosue Tool, through the data processing stage into the storage mart, through the python utilities into required outputs)
  • Maintain and update python utilities when required due to changes from the research team
  • Manage and rectify when failures occur in the pipeline, liaising with both the data providers, research team and developers on the dashboard
  • Manage the storage mart and data warehouse
  • Support team with edits in-house platform

Data Quality Checks:
Assist in performing detailed quality control checks on large and complex datasets to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency.

Data Transformation & Standardisation:
Support the transformation and standardisation of data files to align with internal data structures, project needs, and client-specific formats.

Dashboard Support & Maintenance:
Assist in the creation, population, and maintenance of dashboards using off-the-shelf tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms), ensuring outputs are clear, accurate, and aligned with research objectives.

Data Delivery Management:
Help manage and organise the final delivery of data files and dashboards to internal teams and clients, ensuring timely and error-free execution.

Collaboration Across Teams:
Work closely with internal stakeholders including Development team, Data Processing, Scripting, Project Management and Research teams to ensure smooth handovers and integration of deliverables.

Documentation & Reporting:
Maintain clear documentation of processes, data transformation steps, and QC logs to support transparency, reproducibility, and audit-readiness.

Continuous Learning & Development:
Engage in training opportunities to build knowledge in healthcare data, market research workflows, dashboarding tools, and data handling best practices.

What you'll need

Your skills and experience:

Technical Expertise

  • Atleast 5+ years expertise in languages like Python and SQL.
  • Mastery in advanced Core Python (generators, decorators, OOP, async programming), architecture design.
  • Proficient in libraries like Pandas - Joins, Melting, handling missing values, NumPy, TensorFlow etc.
  • Proficient in SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL).
  • Understanding MVC/MVT, microservices, and system design principles.
  • Experience in setup of data pipelines is highly desirable.
  • Knowledge of high-performance framework (Django, FastAPI, Flask) is a plus.
  • Cloud platform expertise (AWS, Azure, GCP), and building ETL/ELT pipelines.
  • Experience in version control system.

 

Ideal candidate should be

  • You are proactive, dedicated and enthusiastic, with a ‘can do’ attitude and approach
  • You demonstrate a high level of accuracy and attention to detail, as well as strengths in organisation and prioritisation
  • You are a collaborative team player who works well with colleagues
  • You communicate ideas and issues in an effective, straightforward fashion
  • Logical thinking and ability to troubleshoot errors or discrepancies in tabulated data.
  • Ability to interpret tabulation specifications and apply them accurately.
  • Problem-solving skills (particularly relating to situations requiring analytical judgement and establishing best practice solutions)
  • You are highly motivated and want to seize opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career

 

Artificial Intelligence

Inizio may use AI technologies to support certain aspects of our recruitment process, but all hiring decisions are made by our human recruiting team. We are committed to fair, inclusive, and human-led hiring practices. Don't meet every requirement? That's okay! We are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.

Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.

items.title: Compliance Analyst
items.description:
About AlphaSense: 

The world’s most sophisticated companies rely on AlphaSense to remove uncertainty from decision-making. With market intelligence and search built on proven AI, AlphaSense delivers insights that matter from content you can trust. Our universe of public and private content includes equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, trade journals, and clients’ own research content.

The acquisition of Tegus by AlphaSense in 2024 advances our shared mission to empower professionals to make smarter decisions through AI-driven market intelligence. Together, AlphaSense and Tegus will accelerate growth, innovation, and content expansion, with complementary product and content capabilities that enable users to unearth even more comprehensive insights from thousands of content sets. Our platform is trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with more than 2,000 employees across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland. Come join us!

About AlphaSense:

The world’s most sophisticated companies rely on AlphaSense to remove uncertainty from decision-making. With market intelligence and search built on proven AI, AlphaSense delivers insights that matter from content you can trust. Our universe of public and private content includes equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, trade journals, and clients’ own research content. Our platform is trusted by over 4,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500.

Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with over 1,300 people across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, and Singapore. For more information, please visit www.alpha-sense.com. 

About the Team:

Tegus by AlphaSense facilitates extremely valuable calls between our clients and experts. Given the proprietary insights in these conversations, compliance is critical to our business, and a key component of our value add. The Compliance Team provides our clients with actionable insights while maintaining the highest legal and regulatory standards. 

 

About the Role:

This role will be part of our growing India Compliance Review team, providing critical coverage across global time zones to help ensure 24x5 compliance review support for our U.S. and international operations. As a Compliance Analyst, you will play a crucial role in supporting our content generation while protecting our clients from potentially sensitive information. You will gain extensive knowledge of Tegus’ cutting edge compliance program to facilitate the review of expert call transcripts. This role is fast-paced, so agility and ability to work under a tight deadline are key.

 

What You’ll Do: 

  • Review expert call transcripts to detect and resolve any instances of material nonpublic and confidential information in accordance with internal protocols, applicable laws/regulations, and industry standards.
  • Complete due diligence review of each call, document any violations comprehensively, and escalate non-compliance with Tegus sourcing guidelines as necessary.
  • Conduct advanced research of financial reports and regulatory filings using internal databases and external resources to determine the extent to which information exists in the public domain.
  • Proactively manage internal and external communications while maintaining efficiency and adaptability in a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment.
  • Perform administrative tasks, including but not limited to, inputting, tracking, and maintaining accurate data on processes, transcripts, and workflows, while identifying opportunities to improve operational efficiency.

Who You Are:

  • 1-2 years of experience in research, compliance, legal support or operations roles — such as  paralegal, legal assistant, analyst, or BPO/editorial specialist or similar — is preferred, but not required.
  • Acute attention to detail and strong written and verbal fluency in English and reading comprehension with proofreading, drafting, and investigative/research experience.
  • Ability to effectively prioritize tasks, manage time, and remain highly organized while working independently.
  • Thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, with the ability to adapt quickly to changing priorities and contribute to innovative solutions that support Tegus’ business needs.

AlphaSense is an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to a work environment that supports, inspires, and respects all individuals. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling AlphaSense’s commitment to equal employment opportunity. AlphaSense does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of race, color, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, religion, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, military or veteran status, disability, or any other non-merit factor. This policy applies to every aspect of employment at AlphaSense, including recruitment, hiring, training, advancement, and termination.

In addition, it is the policy of AlphaSense to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances where a particular employee works.

Recruiting Scams and Fraud

We at AlphaSense have been made aware of fraudulent job postings and individuals impersonating AlphaSense recruiters. These scams may involve fake job offers, requests for sensitive personal information, or demands for payment. Please note:

  • AlphaSense never asks candidates to pay for job applications, equipment, or training.
  • All official communications will come from an @alpha-sense.com email address.
  • If you’re unsure about a job posting or recruiter, verify it on our Careers page.

If you believe you’ve been targeted by a scam or have any doubts regarding the authenticity of any job listing purportedly from or on behalf of AlphaSense please contact us. Your security and trust matter to us.

As a Compliance Analyst, you will play a crucial role in supporting our content generation while protecting our clients from potentially sensitive information. You will gain extensive knowledge of Tegus’ cutting edge compliance program to facilitate the review of expert call transcripts. This role is fast-paced, so agility and ability to work under a tight deadline are key.

 

What You’ll Do: 

  • Review expert call transcripts to detect and resolve any instances of material nonpublic and confidential information in accordance with internal protocols, applicable laws/regulations, and industry standards.
  • Complete due diligence review of each call, document any violations comprehensively, and escalate non-compliance with Tegus sourcing guidelines as necessary.
  • Conduct advanced research of financial reports and regulatory filings using internal databases and external resources to determine the extent to which information exists in the public domain.
  • Proactively manage internal and external communications while maintaining efficiency and adaptability in a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment.
  • Perform administrative tasks, including but not limited to, inputting, tracking, and maintaining accurate data on processes, transcripts, and workflows, while identifying opportunities to improve operational efficiency.

Who You Are:

  • 1-2 years of experience in research, compliance, legal support or operations roles — such as  paralegal, legal assistant, analyst, or BPO/editorial specialist or similar — is preferred, but not required.
  • Acute attention to detail and strong written and verbal fluency in English and reading comprehension with proofreading, drafting, and investigative/research experience.
  • Ability to effectively prioritize tasks, manage time, and remain highly organized while working independently.
  • Thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, with the ability to adapt quickly to changing priorities and contribute to innovative solutions that support Tegus’ business needs.

 

AlphaSense is an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to a work environment that supports, inspires, and respects all individuals. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling AlphaSense’s commitment to equal employment opportunity. AlphaSense does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of race, color, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, religion, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, military or veteran status, disability, or any other non-merit factor. This policy applies to every aspect of employment at AlphaSense, including recruitment, hiring, training, advancement, and termination.

In addition, it is the policy of AlphaSense to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances where a particular employee works.

Recruiting Scams and Fraud

We at AlphaSense have been made aware of fraudulent job postings and individuals impersonating AlphaSense recruiters. These scams may involve fake job offers, requests for sensitive personal information, or demands for payment. Please note:

  • AlphaSense never asks candidates to pay for job applications, equipment, or training.
  • All official communications will come from an @alpha-sense.com email address.
  • If you’re unsure about a job posting or recruiter, verify it on our Careers page.

If you believe you’ve been targeted by a scam or have any doubts regarding the authenticity of any job listing purportedly from or on behalf of AlphaSense please contact us. Your security and trust matter to us.
